Day Significane:
Losar – Lunar New Year (Start of Chötrul Düchen)
The first through fifteenth days of this lunar month are known as Chötrul Düchen, a sacred period commemorating the time when Buddha Shakyamuni manifested a different miracle each day to inspire faith and accumulate merit.

1st Event: Tibetan New Year (Losar)
1st Event's Details:
Om! To the deities of the wondrous and auspicious realms,
I prostrate with devotion; please hold me with your compassion.
May the two benefits be spontaneously accomplished, and marvelous auspiciousness arise.
May we be enriched by the glory of virtue, excellence, benefit, and bliss.
May the unchanging Tree of Enlightenment always flourish.
May all thoughts in accordance with the Dharma be fulfilled!
Samaya. This was transcribed onto miraculous paper in the small script of [Yeshe] Tsogyal.
May it meet with the destined emanation of the Dro-mi (nomad) through auspicious coincidence.
This is from the profound Dharma of Sangdak Dorje Dradul (also known as Mingyur Dorje), the heart-son of Garwang Dudjom Lingpa.

2nd Event: Lama Losar (New Year of the Guru)
2nd Event's Details:
The first day is the most significant day, representing faith and auspiciousness.
* Waking up early: People rise before dawn, dress in their finest traditional clothes (Chuba), and perform incense offerings (Sang-sol) to the deities.
* Fetching the First Water: Very early in the morning, the "first water" (Chu-phud) is fetched from a spring or well to be placed on the altar as an offering.
* Making Offerings and Paying Respects: Families gather to enjoy traditional foods like Tsampa and Droma Dresi (sweet buttered rice with small tubers). Afterward, they visit monasteries and temples to pray and make offerings.
* Family Gathering: According to tradition, the first day is reserved for immediate family. People generally stay home together rather than visiting relatives or friends on this day.
Preparations Before the New Year
Before Losar begins, the following items must be prepared:
* Chemar and Droso Chemar: Preparing the auspicious "five-grain bucket" (Chemar) filled with roasted barley flour, butter, and seeds, decorated with colorful butter sculptures.
* Making Khapse: Frying various shapes of traditional Tibetan deep-fried pastries (Khapse).
* House Cleaning: Thoroughly cleaning the entire house to sweep away the bad luck of the previous year.
* New Clothes: Making or buying new clothes for the whole family to wear during the celebrations.

3rd Event: Chotrul Duchen: The Festival of Miracles
3rd Event's Details:
Chotrul Duchen is one of the four great festivals of Tibetan Buddhism. It commemorates the special occasion when Buddha Shakyamuni defeated six rival teachers (non-Buddhist masters) through a series of miracles performed at Shravasti.
1. Historical Background
From the 1st to the 15th day of the first month of the Tibetan calendar, the Buddha displayed various miracles for fifteen days to overcome the influence of rival teachers. The 15th day, in particular, marks the culmination of these great miraculous displays, which is why it is called the "Festival of Miracles."
2. Spiritual Benefits
According to Buddhist scriptures, any karmic actions—virtuous or non-virtuous—committed during this period are significantly magnified.
 * 100,000-Fold Merit: It is believed that virtuous deeds performed during this festival—such as making aspirations, performing circumambulations (kora), or reciting mantras—carry a power and benefit 100,000 times greater than usual.
 * Accumulation and Purification: It is considered the most auspicious time to purify the obscurations of ignorance and accumulate vast merit.
3. Traditions and Observances
 * Offerings: Elaborate offerings are made, including butter lamps, food (torma), and flowers.
 * The Great Prayer Festival (Monlam Chenmo): In Tibet, the tradition of the "Lhasa Monlam Chenmo" was established by Je Tsongkhapa at the Lhasa Tsuklakhang to mark this occasion.
 * Butter Sculptures: On the night of the 15th, there is a tradition of displaying and offering intricate Butter Sculptures (Chotrul Tupa).
A Concise Prayer of Benefit
The following verse is used to praise Buddha Shakyamuni during this festival:
> "To the Lord of Sages, who displayed miracles at Shravasti,
> And utterly defeated all rival challengers;
> To the one who became the field of merit for all beings,
> To the Buddha of Shravasti, I prostrate."
3rd Event's Details\n(Tibetan):

བོད་ཟླ་དང་པོའི་ཚེས་ ༡ ནས་ ༡༥ བར་ཉིན་བཅོ་ལྔའི་རིང་ལ། སྟོན་པ་སངས་རྒྱས་ཀྱིས་ཆོ་འཕྲུལ་མི་འདྲ་བ་བསྟན་ནས་མུ་སྟེགས་པའི་སྟོན་པ་རྣམས་ཚར་བཅད། ལྷག་པར་དུ་ཚེས་ ༡༥ ཉིན་ནི་ཆོ་འཕྲུལ་ཆེན་པོ་བསྟན་པའི་དུས་ཚོད་གནད་འགག་ཅིག་ཡིན་པས། འདིར་ "ཆོ་འཕྲུལ་དུས་ཆེན་" ཞེས་བརྗོད།
༢. དུས་ཆེན་འདིའི་ཕན་ཡོན།
ནང་པ་སངས་རྒྱས་པའི་གསུང་རབ་ལྟར་ན། དུས་ཆེན་འདིའི་རིང་ལ་དགེ་སྡིག་གི་ལས་གང་བསགས་པ་ཐམས་ཅད་འགྱུར་ཁྱད་ཆེན་པོ་ཡོད་པར་བཤད།
 * དགེ་བ་འབུམ་འགྱུར། དུས་ཆེན་འདིའི་རིང་ལ་སྨོན་ལམ་འདེབས་པ་དང་། ཕྱག་སྐོར་བྱེད་པ། མ་ཎི་འདོན་པ་སོགས་དགེ་བའི་ལས་ལ་བརྩོན་ན། དེའི་ནུས་པ་དང་ཕན་ཡོན་ནི་དཀྱུས་མ་ལས་ འབུམ་འགྱུར་ (100,000 times) གྱིས་མང་བ་ཡོད་པར་ཡིད་ཆེད་བྱེད།
 * ཚོགས་བསགས་སྒྲིབ་སྦྱོང་། རང་གི་མ་རིག་པའི་སྒྲིབ་པ་སྦྱོང་བ་དང་། བསོད་ནམས་ཀྱི་ཚོགས་གསོག་པའི་དུས་ཚོད་བཟང་ཤོས་ཤིག་ཡིན།
༣. དུས་ཆེན་སྲུང་བརྩི་བྱེད་སྟངས།
 * མཆོད་པ་བཤམས་པ། མར་མེ་དང་། ཞལ་ཟས། མེ་ཏོག་སོགས་ཀྱིས་མཆོད་པ་རྒྱ་ཆེན་པོ་འབུལ་བ།
 * སྨོན་ལམ་ཆེན་མོ། བོད་དུ་ལྷ་ས་གཙུག་ལག་ཁང་དུ་རྗེ་ཙོང་ཁ་པ་ཆེན་པོས་སྲོལ་གཏོད་པའི་ "ལྷ་ས་སྨོན་ལམ་ཆེན་མོ་" ཚོགས་སྲོལ་ཡོད།
 * མར་ཚལ་མཆོད་པ། ཚེས་ ༡༥ ནུབ་མོ་མར་གྱིས་བཟོས་པའི་མཆོད་པ་ (Butter Sculptures) བཤམས་ནས་མཆོད་འབུལ་བྱེད་པའི་ལམ་སྲོལ་ཡོད།
ཕན་ཐོགས་པའི་སྨོན་ལམ་མདོར་བསྡུས།
དུས་ཆེན་འདིར་སྟོན་པ་བཅོམ་ལྡན་འདས་ལ་བསྟོད་པ་བྱེད་པའི་ཚིག་ནི།
> "ཐུབ་པའི་དབང་པོ་མཉན་ཡོད་ཆོ་འཕྲུལ་བསྟན། །མུ་སྟེགས་ཕས་རྒོལ་མཐའ་དག་ཕམ་མཛད་དེ། །འགྲོ་བ་ཀུན་གྱི་བསོད་ནམས་ཞིང་གྱུར་པ། །མཉན་ཡོད་ཐུབ་པའི་ཞབས་ལ་ཕྱག་འཚལ་ལོ། །"

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to study, reflect and meditate, Tirelessly, both day and night, without ever straying into idleness, In order to free oneself and others from this ocean of saṃsāra, Having gained this supreme vessel—a free, well-favoured human life, so difficult to find.
Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings thieves, robbers, grief, and sorrow.
1st Event: Gyalpo Losar (King's New Year)
1st Event's Details:
This day is dedicated to visiting and exchanging greetings with relatives and friends.
* Visiting Relatives: People start going out to visit the homes of relatives, friends, and teachers to celebrate the New Year together.
* Tashi Delek (Auspicious Greetings): People offer each other ceremonial scarves (Khata), present the auspicious Chemar (barley flour and butter offering), and offer traditional barley beer (Chang) as a welcome.
* Enjoying Feasts: Large meals are hosted where guests enjoy a variety of Tibetan dishes, particularly traditional Tibetan butter tea and barley beer, in a joyful atmosphere.
1st Event's Details (Tibetan):
ཉིན་འདི་ནི་གཉེན་འབྲེལ་དང་མཛའ་གྲོགས་བར་འཚམས་འདྲི་བྱེད་པའི་ཉིན་མོ་དེ་ཡིན།
* གཉེན་འཕྲད་དུ་འགྲོ་བ། གཉེན་ཉེ་དང་། གྲོགས་པོ། དགེ་རྒན་སོགས་ཀྱི་ནང་དུ་ལོ་གསར་ལ་འགྲོ་བའི་འགོ་འཛུགས་ཀྱི་རེད།
* བཀྲ་ཤིས་བདེ་ལེགས། ཕན་ཚུན་ལ་ཁ་བཏགས་སྒྲོན་པ་དང་། གྲོ་སོ་ཕྱེ་མར་འདེགས་པ། ཆང་སྐྱེམས་འདྲེན་པ་སོགས་བྱེད།
* གསོལ་སྟོན་ལ་རོལ་བ། མགྲོན་པོ་བཤམས་ནས་བོད་ཀྱི་ཟས་རིགས་སྣ་ཚོགས་དང་ལྷག་པར་དུ་བོད་ཇ་དང་ཆང་ལ་རོལ་ནས་སྐྱིད་པོ་བྱེད།

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to leave behind one’s homeland, Where our attachment to family and friends overwhelms us like a torrent, While our aversion towards enemies rages inside us like a blazing fire, And delusion’s darkness obscures what must be adopted and abandoned.
Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and suffering to others.
1st Event: Incense Offering (Sangsol) at the local Tenkhar
1st Event's Details:
The Source of Happiness and Fortune
A Concise Smoke Offering to Gesar
by Sera Khandro
 
om ah hung
oṃ āḥ hūṃ
zakmé yeshe dütsi sang chö di
I present this smoke offering of pure wisdom nectar
rik sum sempé gyutrul sengchen gyal
To the Great Lion, King Gesar, magical emanation of the bodhisattvas of the Three Families,
kakhor lejé sungmé tsok la bul
And to the assembly of servants and guardians.
zhé la sampé dön kün drubpar dzö
Please accept it and accomplish all our wishes.
 
Thus, prompted by laziness, this concise text was written by the one named Dewé Dorje during the accumulation of a specific number of recitations.
1st Event's Details (Tibetan):
"གེ་སར་བསང་བསྡུས་བདེ་ལེགས་འབྱུང་གནས་བཞུགས༔
 
ཨོཾ་ཨཱཿཧཱུྃ༔
ཟག་མེད་ཡེ་ཤེས་བདུད་རྩིའི་བསང་མཆོད་འདི༔
རིག་གསུམ་སེམས་དཔའི་སྒྱུ་འཕྲུལ་སེང་ཆེན་རྒྱལ༔
བཀའ་འཁོར་ལས་བྱེད་སྲུང་མའི་ཚོགས་ལ་འབུལ༔
བཞེས་ལ་བསམ་པའི་དོན་ཀུན་འགྲུབ་པར་མཛོད༔
 ཅེས་བདེ་བའི་རྡོ་རྗེའི་མིང་གིས་གྲངས་ཚད་གསོག་སྐབས་ལེ་ལོས་བསྐུལ་ནས་ཉུང་བསྡུས་འདི་བྲིས་པའོ། །
གེ་སར་བསང་བསྡུས་བདེ་ལེགས་འབྱུང་གནས་བཞུགས༔"
2nd Event: Chos-Kyong Losar (New Year of the Protectors)
2nd Event's Details:
This is the day dedicated to making offerings to the Dharma Protectors and accumulating merit.
* Changing Prayer Flags: People hoist new prayer flags (Dar-chog) on their rooftops and mountain tops. They also perform elaborate incense burning ceremonies (Sang-sol).
* Chanting "Lha-Gyal-Lo": Large groups of people gather to shout "Kye Kye Lha Gyal Lo!" (Victory to the Deities!), throwing roasted barley flour into the air as an auspicious prayer for success and protection.
* Festivities and Entertainment: From this day onward, public celebrations increase with traditional folk songs, dances, and various forms of community entertainment.
2nd Event's Details\n(Tibetan):
ཉིན་འདི་ནི་གཉེན་འབྲེལ་དང་མཛའ་གྲོགས་བར་འཚམས་འདྲི་བྱེད་པའི་ཉིན་མོ་དེ་ཡིན།
* གཉེན་འཕྲད་དུ་འགྲོ་བ། གཉེན་ཉེ་དང་། གྲོགས་པོ། དགེ་རྒན་སོགས་ཀྱི་ནང་དུ་ལོ་གསར་ལ་འགྲོ་བའི་འགོ་འཛུགས་ཀྱི་རེད།
* བཀྲ་ཤིས་བདེ་ལེགས། ཕན་ཚུན་ལ་ཁ་བཏགས་སྒྲོན་པ་དང་། གྲོ་སོ་ཕྱེ་མར་འདེགས་པ། ཆང་སྐྱེམས་འདྲེན་པ་སོགས་བྱེད།
* གསོལ་སྟོན་ལ་རོལ་བ། མགྲོན་པོ་བཤམས་ནས་བོད་ཀྱི་ཟས་རིགས་སྣ་ཚོགས་དང་ལྷག་པར་དུ་བོད་ཇ་དང་ཆང་ལ་རོལ་ནས་སྐྱིད་པོ་བྱེད།
3rd Event: Parinirvana of H.H. Minling Trichen Rinpoche (Mindrolling lineage)

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to take to solitary places, Avoiding the unwholesome, so that destructive emotions gradually fade away, And, in the absence of distraction, virtuous practice naturally gains strength; Whilst, with awareness clearly focused, we gain conviction in the teachings.
Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations and new clothes.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to renounce this life’s concerns, For friends and relatives, long acquainted, must all go their separate ways; Wealth and prized possessions, painstakingly acquired, must all be left behind; And consciousness, the guest who lodges in the body, must in time depart.
Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will also take the lives of others.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to avoid destructive friends, In whose company the three poisons of the mind grow stronger, And we engage less and less in study, reflection and meditation, So that love and compassion fade away until they are no more.
Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations with teachers/relatives; brings dissension.

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A day dedicated to healing prayers, longevity practices, and the cultivation of compassion for those who are ill.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane:
Bhagavan, whose compassion is equal toward all,
Just hearing your name clears the suffering of lower realms;
Medicine Buddha, healer of the diseases of the Three Poisons,
To you, the Light of Lapis Lazuli, I prostrate.
Meaning of Coincidence: Ample Food/Clothing: Good for giving generously and wealth practices.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cherish spiritual friends, By regarding them as even more precious than one’s own body, Since they are the ones who will help to rid us of all our faults, And make our virtues grow ever greater just like the waxing moon.
Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will also take the lives of others.
Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A sacred day to honor Guru Padmasambhava, strengthen guru devotion, and renew Vajrayana commitments.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane:
Guru Loden Choksé
On the tenth day of the dragon month, you are ordained by Ānanda,
And with countless scholars and adepts who have achieved the ultimate in knowledge,
You attain consummate mastery over all the sūtras and tantras,
And are famed as both Shakya Senge and Loden Choksé—at your feet we pray.
 Grant your blessing so that the lords of death, planetary demons and the eight classes who bring harm all are pacified,
Pure discipline and the three higher trainings are perfected,
And our knowledge of every field of learning, and of all the sūtras and tantras,
Grows to become limitless.
Unnamed: 25:
འབྲུག་ཟླའི་ཚེས་བཅུར་ཀུན་དགར་རབ་ཏུ་བྱུང༌། །
ཤེས་བྱ་མཐར་ཕྱིན་མཁས་གྲུབ་དུ་མ་ལས། །
མདོ་སྔགས་ཀུན་ཆུབ་ཤཱཀྱ་སེང་གེར་གྲགས། །
བློ་ལྡན་མཆོག་སྲེད་ཞབས་ལ་གསོལ་བ་འདེབས། །
འཆི་བདག་གཟའ་བདུད་སྡེ་བརྒྱད་གནོད་འཚེ་ཞི། །
ཚུལ་ཁྲིམས་རྣམ་དག་བསླབ་གསུམ་ཡོངས་སུ་རྫོགས། །
རིག་པའི་གནས་དང་མདོ་རྒྱུད་ཐམས་ཅད་ལ། །
མཁྱེན་རབ་ཕྱོགས་མེད་རྒྱས་པར་བྱིན་གྱིས་རློབས། །
Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings thieves, robbers, and grief.
1st Event: Grand Display of the Guru Rinpoche Thangka (Appliqué scroll)

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to take refuge in the Three Jewels, Since they will never fail to provide protection for all who call upon them, For whom are the ordinary gods of this world ever capable of helping, As long as they themselves are trapped within saṃsāra’s vicious cycle?
Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings thieves, robbers, and grief.
1st Event: Parinirvana of H.H. Jamyang Khyentse Wangpo (Sakya lineage)

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is never to commit a harmful act, Even though not to do so might put one’s very life at risk, For the Sage himself has taught how negative actions will ripen Into the manifold miseries of the lower realms, so difficult to endure.
Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings thieves, robbers, and grief.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to strive towards the goal, Which is the supreme state of changeless, everlasting liberation, Since all the happiness of the three realms lasts but a moment, And then is quickly gone, just like dewdrops on blades of grass.
Meaning of Coincidence: Ample Food/Clothing: Good for giving generously and wealth practices.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to arouse bodhicitta, So as to bring freedom to all sentient beings, infinite in number. For how can true happiness ever be found while our mothers, Who have cared for us throughout the ages, endure such pain?
Meaning of Coincidence: Ambrosia: Strengthens healthy long life; good for business.

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A powerful day for merit-making, purification, offerings, and strengthening positive intentions.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane: "The Day of Amitabha Buddha"
Meaning of Coincidence: Ample Food/Clothing: Good for giving generously and wealth practices.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to make a genuine exchange Of one’s own happiness and well-being for all the sufferings of others. Since all misery comes from seeking happiness for oneself alone, Whilst perfect buddhahood is born from the wish for others’ good.
Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will also take the lives of others.

Day Significane: Even if others, in the grips of great desire, should steal, Or encourage others to take away, all the wealth that I possess, To dedicate to them entirely my body, possessions and all my merits From the past, present and future— this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Certainty: Fulfills wishes speedily; good for resolving disputes.

Day Significane: Even if others should seek to cut off my head, Though I’ve done them not the slightest wrong, To take upon myself, out of compassion, All the harms they have amassed—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings grief and sorrow.

Day Significane: Even if others should declare before the world All manner of unpleasant things about me, To speak only of their qualities in return, With a mind that’s filled with love—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings grief and sorrow.
Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

Day Significane: Even if others should expose my hidden faults or deride me When speaking amidst great gatherings of many people, To conceive of them as spiritual friends and to bow Before them in respect—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for making offerings.
1st Event: Annual Great Accomplishment Ceremony (Drubchen)

Day Significane: Even if others whom I have cared for like children of my own Should turn upon me and treat me as an enemy, To regard them only with special fondness and affection, As a mother would her ailing child—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings dissension and disputes.

Day Significane: Even if others, equal or inferior to me in status, Should, out of arrogance, disparage me, To honour them, as I would my teacher, By bowing down my head before them—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Ample Food/Clothing: Good for giving generously and wealth practices.

Day Significane: Even though I may be destitute and despised by all, Beset with terrible illness and plagued by evil spirits, Still to take upon myself all beings’ ills and harmful actions, Without ever losing heart—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

Day Significane: Even though I may be famous and revered by all, And as rich as Vaiśravaṇa, the god of wealth himself, To see the futility of all the glory and riches of this world And to remain without conceit—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings thieves and robbers.

Mother of diversity, Prajñāpāramitā,
Padma's consort, joyful, passionate and smiling—
Yeshe Tsogyal, homage to you!
Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to subdue the mind With the forces of loving kindness and compassion. For unless the real adversary—my own anger—is defeated, Outer enemies, though I may conquer them, will continue to appear.
Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to turn away immediately From those things which bring desire and attachment. For the pleasures of the senses are just like salty water: The more we taste of them, the more our thirst increases.
Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and suffering to others.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is never to entertain concepts, Which revolve around dualistic notions of perceiver and perceived, In the knowledge that all these appearances are but the mind itself, Whilst mind’s own nature is forever beyond the limitations of ideas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

"བོད་ཟླ་རེའི་ཚེས་ ༢༩ ཉིན་ནི་*ཆོས་སྐྱོང་སྲུང་མའི་དུས་ཆེན་ནམ་དུས་བཟང་*གལ་ཆེན་ཞིག་ཡིན། 
༡། གོ་དོན།
བོད་ཀྱི་ལོ་ཐོའི་ནང་ཟླ་རེའི་ཚེས་ ༢༩ ནི་*ཡི་དམ་དང་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་*རྣམས་ཀྱི་དུས་བཟང་དུ་བརྩི་བ་ཡིན། ལྷག་པར་དུ་ལོ་མཇུག་གི་དགུ་གཏོར་དང་། ཟླ་རེའི་ནང་གི་ནག་པོའི་ཕྱོགས་ཀྱི་མཐའ་མར་ཉེ་བའི་སྐབས་འདིར། དྲག་པོའི་ལས་སྒྲུབ་པ་དང་བར་ཆད་བཟློག་པའི་དུས་ཁྱད་པར་ཅན་ཞིག་ཏུ་ངོས་འཛིན་བྱེད།
༢། བྱེད་སྒོ།
ཉིན་འདིར་དགོན་སྡེ་ཁག་དང་ཁྱིམ་ཚང་སོ་སོའི་ནང་དུ།
 * གསོལ་ཁ་འབུལ་བ། ཆོས་སྐྱོང་སྲུང་མ་རྣམས་ལ་གཏོར་མ་དང་། གསེར་སྐྱེམས། བསངས་མཆོད་སོགས་རྒྱ་ཆེན་པོ་འབུལ་བ།
 * བསྐང་བཤགས་གཏོང་བ། རང་གི་དམ་ཚིག་ཉམས་ཆག་རྣམས་བཤགས་ཤིང་། སྲུང་མའི་ཐུགས་དམ་བསྐང་བའི་ཆོ་ག་བྱེད་པ།
༣། ཕན་ཡོན།
ཚེས་ ༢༩ ལ་སྲུང་མ་མཆོད་པའི་ཕན་ཡོན་ནི་བསམ་གྱིས་མི་ཁྱབ་པ་ཡོད་དེ།
 * བར་ཆད་ཞི་བ། ལམ་ལྷོངས་མ་ཡོང་བའི་གེགས་བར་དང་། མི་ཁ་ཁ་མཆུ། གནོད་པ་ཐམས་ཅད་ཞི་བར་བྱེད།
 * དམ་ཚིག་གཙང་མར་བཟོ་བ། བླ་མ་དང་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་དབར་གྱི་འགལ་འཁྲུལ་དང་དམ་ཚིག་ཉམས་པ་རྣམས་སོར་ཆུད་པར་བྱེད།
 * བྱ་བ་ལམ་འགྲོ། ཆོས་བཞིན་སྒྲུབ་པའི་ལས་དོན་ཐམས་ཅད་ལ་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་གིས་མགོན་སྐྱོབ་དང་ཕྱྭ་གཡང་སྩོལ་བར་བྱེད།
 * བསྟན་པ་སྲུང་བ། སྒེར་གྱི་དོན་ཙམ་མ་ཡིན་པར་སངས་རྒྱས་ཀྱི་བསྟན་པ་དང་འཛམ་གླིང་ཞི་བདེ་སྲུང་བའི་ནུས་པ་སྤེལ།
༤། དམིགས་བསལ་གྱི་དོན་སྙིང་།
སྲུང་མ་མཆོད་པ་ནི་ལྷ་གསོལ་བ་ཙམ་མ་ཡིན་པར། རང་གི་སེམས་ནང་གི་*དྲག་པོའི་ནུས་པ་*འཕེལ་བར་བྱས་ཏེ། ནང་གི་ཉོན་མོངས་པའི་དགྲ་བཅོམ་པ་དང་། ཕྱིའི་འཇིག་རྟེན་གྱི་བྱ་བ་གང་ཡང་བརྟན་པོར་སྐྱོང་བའི་སྙིང་སྟོབས་བསྐྱེད་པ་ལ་གོ་དགོས།
> ལམ་སྟོན། ཉིན་འདིར་ཁྱེད་ཀྱིས་རང་གི་ཡི་དམ་མམ་སྲུང་མའི་མ་ཎི་དང་སྙིང་པོ་བཟླས་པ་དང་། གཙང་བྲ་ལ་གཟབ་ནས་གསེར་སྐྱེམས་འབུལ་ན་ཤིན་ཏུ་ལེགས།"

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A day of introspection and renewal, traditionally used for purification practices and restoring commitments.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane:
"The Day of the Hundred Sacred Peaceful and Wrathful Deities"

Confession of Negative Actions (Digpa Shagpa): As it is the final day of the month, it is a day for purifying and confessing the non-virtuous actions committed throughout that month.
Restoration and Purification (Sojong): It is also the day when the monastic community (Sangha) gathers to perform the "Sojong" ritual, a ceremony for restoring vows and purifying transgressions according to the Vinaya (monastic discipline).
Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.
1st Event: The Audience of the Eight Manifestations of Guru Rinpoche Cham Dance
1st Event's Details:
The sacred Cham dance of the Eight Manifestations of Guru Rinpoche, arising from the lineage of Dechen Lingpa and Dakla Gampo’s Ogyen Drodrul Lingpa, is known to possess a spiritual power and blessing far greater than others. Below is a brief explanation of its history and benefits.
Characteristics of the Lineage
This lineage stems from a continuous succession of Pure Visions (Daknang) and Revealed Treasures (Terma). Therefore, the movements of the dance, the melodies, and the costumes are said to be in perfect accordance with the enlightened intention of Guru Rinpoche himself.
Benefits of Witnessing the Dance
Within the non-sectarian traditions of Tibetan Buddhism, this Cham dance is referred to as "Liberation through Seeing" (Thongdrol), meaning that simply beholding it with one’s eyes has the power to plant the seed of liberation.
* Clearing All Obstacles: As the dance is inseparable from the actual presence of Guru Rinpoche, it has the benefit of averting obstacles caused by humans and non-humans, as well as warding off epidemics and untimely death.
* Purification of Negativities: If witnessed with one-pointed faith, the negative karma and obscurations accumulated since beginningless lifetimes will be purified.
* Receiving Blessings in One’s Being: Each movement of the Eight Manifestations creates the auspicious connection for the five poisons to be liberated into their natural state and for primordial wisdom to arise within the mind-stream.
* Rebirth in the Pure Land: Through the merit of witnessing this dance, one establishes a profound connection to be reborn in the copper-colored mountain pure land (Zangdok Palri) in the future.
1st Event's Details (Tibetan):
བདེ་ཆེན་གླིང་པ་དང་དྭགས་ལ་སྒམ་པོའི་ཨོ་རྒྱན་འགྲོ་འདུལ་གླིང་པའི་བརྒྱུད་པ་ལས་བྱུང་བའི་གུ་རུ་མཚན་བརྒྱད་ཀྱི་གར་འཆམས་ནི་བྱིན་རླབས་ཀྱི་ཚན་ཁ་གཞན་ལས་ཆེ་བ་ཞིག་ཡིན། འདིའི་ལོ་རྒྱུས་དང་ཕན་ཡོན་གྱི་འགྲེལ་པ་རགས་བསྡུས་ཤིག་གཤམ་དུ་བཀོད་ཡོད།
 བརྒྱུད་པའི་ཁྱད་ཆོས། འདི་ནི་དག་སྣང་དང་གཏེར་མའི་བརྒྱུད་པ་ཟམ་མ་ཆད་པ་ཞིག་ཡིན་པས། འཆམས་ཀྱི་སྟངས་སྟབས་དང་། དབྱངས་རྟ། ཆས་གོས་སོགས་གུ་རུ་རིན་པོ་ཆེ་རང་ཉིད་ཀྱི་དགོངས་པ་དང་མཐུན་པར་བཤད།
མཇལ་ཁའི་ཕན་ཡོན།
བོད་ཀྱི་ཆོས་རྒྱུད་རིས་མེད་ནང་གར་འཆམས་འདི་ལ་མཐོང་གྲོལ་ཞེས་བརྗོད་དེ། མིག་གིས་མཐོང་བ་ཙམ་གྱིས་ཐར་པའི་ས་བོན་འཇོག་པའི་ནུས་པ་ཡོད།
* བར་ཆད་ཀུན་སེལ། གུ་རུ་རིན་པོ་ཆེའི་སྐུ་དངོས་སུ་བྱོན་པ་དང་གཉིས་སུ་མེད་པས། མི་དང་མི་མ་ཡིན་པའི་བར་ཆད་དང་། ནད་ཡམས་དུས་མིན་གྱི་འཆི་བ་སོགས་བཟློག་པའི་ཕན་ཡོན་ཡོད།
* སྡིག་སྒྲིབ་བྱང་བ། དད་པ་རྩེ་གཅིག་པོས་མཇལ་ན། ཚེ་རབས་ཐོག་མ་མེད་པ་ནས་བསགས་པའི་ལས་ངན་སྡིག་སྒྲིབ་ཐམས་ཅད་འདག་པར་འགྱུར།
* བྱིན་རླབས་རྒྱུད་ལ་འཇུག་པ། གུ་རུ་མཚན་བརྒྱད་ཀྱི་གར་སྟབས་རེ་རེས་དུག་ལྔ་རང་སར་གྲོལ་བ་དང་། ཡེ་ཤེས་ཀྱི་སྣང་བ་རྒྱུད་ལ་སྐྱེ་བའི་རྟེན་འབྲེལ་ཡོད།
* ཞིང་ཁམས་སུ་སྐྱེ་བ། གར་འཆམས་མཇལ་བའི་བསོད་ནམས་ཀྱིས་མ་འོངས་པར་ཟངས་མདོག་དཔལ་རིའི་ཞིང་ཁམས་སུ་སྐྱེ་བའི་སྨོན་ལམ་གྱི་འབྲེལ་བ་ཟབ་མོ་འཇོག་ཐུབ།

1st Event: Parinirvana of H.H. Kyabje Penor Rinpoche (1932–2009)
2nd Event: The Result of Annual Exams for the primary school and Shedra (monastic college) of Odisha Dudjom Vihara

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to recognize delusion Whenever one is confronted by adversity or misfortune. For these sufferings are just like the death of a child in a dream, And it’s so exhausting to cling to delusory perceptions as real.
Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.
1st Event: Parinirvana of H.H. Minling Terchen Rinpoche

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to give out of generosity, With no hopes of karmic recompense or expectation of reward. For if those who seek awakening must give even their own bodies, What need is there to mention mere outer objects and possessions?
Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to observe ethical restraint, Without the slightest intention of continuing in saṃsāric existence. For lacking discipline one will never secure even one’s own well-being, And so any thought of bringing benefit to others would be absurd.
Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cultivate patience, Free from any trace of animosity towards anyone at all, Since any potential source of harm is like a priceless treasure To the bodhisattva who is eager to enjoy a wealth of virtue.
Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will also take the lives of others.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to strive with enthusiastic diligence— The source of all good qualities—when working for the sake of all who live; Seeing that even śrāvakas and pratyekabuddhas, who labour for themselves alone, Exert themselves as if urgently trying to extinguish fires upon their heads.
Meaning of Coincidence: Ample Food/Clothing: Good for wealth practices and sowing seeds.
1st Event: Annual Krodhikali Ghanachakra  (Troma Bhumtshok) (3 days)

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cultivate concentration, Which utterly transcends the four formless absorptions, In the knowledge that mental afflictions are overcome entirely Through penetrating insight suffused with stable calm.
Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A day dedicated to healing prayers, longevity practices, and the cultivation of compassion for those who are ill.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane:
Bhagavan, whose compassion is equal toward all,
Just hearing your name clears the suffering of lower realms;
Medicine Buddha, healer of the diseases of the Three Poisons,
To you, the Light of Lapis Lazuli, I prostrate.
Meaning of Coincidence: Certainty: Fulfills wishes speedily; good for resolving disputes.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cultivate wisdom, Beyond the three conceptual spheres, alongside skilful means, Since it is not possible to attain the perfect level of awakening Through the other five pāramitās alone, in wisdom’s absence.
Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.
Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts
1st Event: Handover of staff/administrative duties at Odisha Dudjom Vihara

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A sacred day to honor Guru Padmasambhava, strengthen guru devotion, and renew Vajrayana commitments.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane:
Guru Chimé Pemajungné
On the tenth day of the snake month, the king of Zahor
Burns you in a fire, which you transform into a lake, emerging resplendent upon the heart of a lotus blossom.
Relying on Princess Mandāravā as consort, you attain the vajra body—
Immortal Lotus-born, to you we pray.
 Grant your blessing so that all harm is eliminated from ill-omened planets and stars, from enemies and thieves,
When at home or abroad, auspiciousness, prosperity and well-being increase,
Well-intentioned guardians act always as our allies and support,
And all our wishes are spontaneously fulfilled.
Unnamed: 25:
སྦྲུལ་ཟླའི་ཚེས་བཅུར་ཟ་ཧོར་རྒྱལ་པོ་ཡིས། །
མེར་བསྲེགས་མཚོར་སྒྱུར་པད་སྡོང་དབུས་སུ་བཞུགས། །
ལྷ་ལྕམ་ཡུམ་བསྟེན་རྡོ་རྗེའི་སྐུ་རུ་གྱུར། །
འཆི་མེད་པདྨ་འབྱུང་གནས་ལ་གསོལ་བ་འདེབས། །
གཟའ་སྐར་ཕྱོགས་ངན་དགྲ་རྐུན་གནོད་འཚེ་ཞི། །
གཞི་བྱེས་ཀུན་ཏུ་བཀྲ་ཤིས་བདེ་ལེགས་འཕེལ། །
དཀར་ཕྱོགས་སྲུང་མས་རྟག་ཏུ་སྡོང་གྲོགས་མཛད། །
བསམ་རྒུ་ལྷུན་གྱིས་འགྲུབ་པར་བྱིན་གྱིས་རློབས། །
Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings grief and sorrow.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to study, reflect and meditate, Tirelessly, both day and night, without ever straying into idleness, In order to free oneself and others from this ocean of saṃsāra, Having gained this supreme vessel—a free, well-favoured human life, so difficult to find.
Meaning of Coincidence: Ample Food/Clothing: Good for giving and sowing seeds.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to leave behind one’s homeland, Where our attachment to family and friends overwhelms us like a torrent, While our aversion towards enemies rages inside us like a blazing fire, And delusion’s darkness obscures what must be adopted and abandoned.
Meaning of Coincidence: Ambrosia: Strengthens long life; good for business.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to take to solitary places, Avoiding the unwholesome, so that destructive emotions gradually fade away, And, in the absence of distraction, virtuous practice naturally gains strength; Whilst, with awareness clearly focused, we gain conviction in the teachings.
Meaning of Coincidence: Ample Food/Clothing: Good for wealth and sowing seeds.

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A powerful day for merit-making, purification, offerings, and strengthening positive intentions.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane: "The Day of Amitabha Buddha"
Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; very negative coincidence.
1st Event: Commencement of Teaching/Classes at Odisha Dudjom Vihara primary school and Shedra

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to renounce this life’s concerns, For friends and relatives, long acquainted, must all go their separate ways; Wealth and prized possessions, painstakingly acquired, must all be left behind; And consciousness, the guest who lodges in the body, must in time depart.
Meaning of Coincidence: Certainty: Fulfills wishes speedily; resolves disputes.

Lunar Status: The (Tibetan Day) is doubled this month
Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to avoid destructive friends, In whose company the three poisons of the mind grow stronger, And we engage less and less in study, reflection and meditation, So that love and compassion fade away until they are no more.
Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings thieves and robbers.

Lunar Status: The (Tibetan day) is doubled this month
Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cherish spiritual friends, By regarding them as even more precious than one’s own body, Since they are the ones who will help to rid us of all our faults, And make our virtues grow ever greater just like the waxing moon.
Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings thieves and robbers.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to take refuge in the Three Jewels, Since they will never fail to provide protection for all who call upon them, For whom are the ordinary gods of this world ever capable of helping, As long as they themselves are trapped within saṃsāra’s vicious cycle?
Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings auspiciousness; good for rituals.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is never to commit a harmful act, Even though not to do so might put one’s very life at risk, For the Sage himself has taught how negative actions will ripen Into the manifold miseries of the lower realms, so difficult to endure.
Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will also take the lives of others.
Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to strive towards the goal, Which is the supreme state of changeless, everlasting liberation, Since all the happiness of the three realms lasts but a moment, And then is quickly gone, just like dewdrops on blades of grass.
Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to arouse bodhicitta, So as to bring freedom to all sentient beings, infinite in number. For how can true happiness ever be found while our mothers, Who have cared for us throughout the ages, endure such pain?
Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to make a genuine exchange Of one’s own happiness and well-being for all the sufferings of others. Since all misery comes from seeking happiness for oneself alone, Whilst perfect buddhahood is born from the wish for others’ good.
Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings dissension.

Day Significane: Even if others, in the grips of great desire, should steal, Or encourage others to take away, all the wealth that I possess, To dedicate to them entirely my body, possessions and all my merits From the past, present and future— this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

Day Significane: Even if others should seek to cut off my head, Though I’ve done them not the slightest wrong, To take upon myself, out of compassion, All the harms they have amassed—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A day connected to wisdom energy and enlightened activity — suitable for mantra recitation and inner practice.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane:
"The Day of the Dakini Festival"                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          Mother of all the victorious ones, Vajrayoginī,
Mother of diversity, Prajñāpāramitā,
Padma's consort, joyful, passionate and smiling—
Yeshe Tsogyal, homage to you!
Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.

Day Significane: Even if others should declare before the world All manner of unpleasant things about me, To speak only of their qualities in return, With a mind that’s filled with love—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

Day Significane: Even if others should expose my hidden faults or deride me When speaking amidst great gatherings of many people, To conceive of them as spiritual friends and to bow Before them in respect—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; very negative coincidence.
1st Event: Dr. Ambedkar's Birthday

Day Significane: Even if others whom I have cared for like children of my own Should turn upon me and treat me as an enemy, To regard them only with special fondness and affection, As a mother would her ailing child—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A day to make offerings and prayers to Dharma Protectors for the removal of obstacles and protection of practice.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane: Day dedicated to invoking Dharma Protectors like Mahakala and Palden Lhamo to remove obstacles and strengthen protection.
Unnamed: 25:
"བོད་ཟླ་རེའི་ཚེས་ ༢༩ ཉིན་ནི་*ཆོས་སྐྱོང་སྲུང་མའི་དུས་ཆེན་ནམ་དུས་བཟང་*གལ་ཆེན་ཞིག་ཡིན། 
༡། གོ་དོན།
བོད་ཀྱི་ལོ་ཐོའི་ནང་ཟླ་རེའི་ཚེས་ ༢༩ ནི་*ཡི་དམ་དང་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་*རྣམས་ཀྱི་དུས་བཟང་དུ་བརྩི་བ་ཡིན། ལྷག་པར་དུ་ལོ་མཇུག་གི་དགུ་གཏོར་དང་། ཟླ་རེའི་ནང་གི་ནག་པོའི་ཕྱོགས་ཀྱི་མཐའ་མར་ཉེ་བའི་སྐབས་འདིར། དྲག་པོའི་ལས་སྒྲུབ་པ་དང་བར་ཆད་བཟློག་པའི་དུས་ཁྱད་པར་ཅན་ཞིག་ཏུ་ངོས་འཛིན་བྱེད།
༢། བྱེད་སྒོ།
ཉིན་འདིར་དགོན་སྡེ་ཁག་དང་ཁྱིམ་ཚང་སོ་སོའི་ནང་དུ།
 * གསོལ་ཁ་འབུལ་བ། ཆོས་སྐྱོང་སྲུང་མ་རྣམས་ལ་གཏོར་མ་དང་། གསེར་སྐྱེམས། བསངས་མཆོད་སོགས་རྒྱ་ཆེན་པོ་འབུལ་བ།
 * བསྐང་བཤགས་གཏོང་བ། རང་གི་དམ་ཚིག་ཉམས་ཆག་རྣམས་བཤགས་ཤིང་། སྲུང་མའི་ཐུགས་དམ་བསྐང་བའི་ཆོ་ག་བྱེད་པ།
༣། ཕན་ཡོན།
ཚེས་ ༢༩ ལ་སྲུང་མ་མཆོད་པའི་ཕན་ཡོན་ནི་བསམ་གྱིས་མི་ཁྱབ་པ་ཡོད་དེ།
 * བར་ཆད་ཞི་བ། ལམ་ལྷོངས་མ་ཡོང་བའི་གེགས་བར་དང་། མི་ཁ་ཁ་མཆུ། གནོད་པ་ཐམས་ཅད་ཞི་བར་བྱེད།
 * དམ་ཚིག་གཙང་མར་བཟོ་བ། བླ་མ་དང་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་དབར་གྱི་འགལ་འཁྲུལ་དང་དམ་ཚིག་ཉམས་པ་རྣམས་སོར་ཆུད་པར་བྱེད།
 * བྱ་བ་ལམ་འགྲོ། ཆོས་བཞིན་སྒྲུབ་པའི་ལས་དོན་ཐམས་ཅད་ལ་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་གིས་མགོན་སྐྱོབ་དང་ཕྱྭ་གཡང་སྩོལ་བར་བྱེད།
 * བསྟན་པ་སྲུང་བ། སྒེར་གྱི་དོན་ཙམ་མ་ཡིན་པར་སངས་རྒྱས་ཀྱི་བསྟན་པ་དང་འཛམ་གླིང་ཞི་བདེ་སྲུང་བའི་ནུས་པ་སྤེལ།
༤། དམིགས་བསལ་གྱི་དོན་སྙིང་།
སྲུང་མ་མཆོད་པ་ནི་ལྷ་གསོལ་བ་ཙམ་མ་ཡིན་པར། རང་གི་སེམས་ནང་གི་*དྲག་པོའི་ནུས་པ་*འཕེལ་བར་བྱས་ཏེ། ནང་གི་ཉོན་མོངས་པའི་དགྲ་བཅོམ་པ་དང་། ཕྱིའི་འཇིག་རྟེན་གྱི་བྱ་བ་གང་ཡང་བརྟན་པོར་སྐྱོང་བའི་སྙིང་སྟོབས་བསྐྱེད་པ་ལ་གོ་དགོས།
> ལམ་སྟོན། ཉིན་འདིར་ཁྱེད་ཀྱིས་རང་གི་ཡི་དམ་མམ་སྲུང་མའི་མ་ཎི་དང་སྙིང་པོ་བཟླས་པ་དང་། གཙང་བྲ་ལ་གཟབ་ནས་གསེར་སྐྱེམས་འབུལ་ན་ཤིན་ཏུ་ལེགས།"
Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings auspiciousness; good for rituals.
Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A day of introspection and renewal, traditionally used for purification practices and restoring commitments.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane:
"The Day of the Hundred Sacred Peaceful and Wrathful Deities"

Confession of Negative Actions (Digpa Shagpa): As it is the final day of the month, it is a day for purifying and confessing the non-virtuous actions committed throughout that month.
Restoration and Purification (Sojong): It is also the day when the monastic community (Sangha) gathers to perform the "Sojong" ritual, a ceremony for restoring vows and purifying transgressions according to the Vinaya (monastic discipline).
Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.
1st Event: Parinirvana of H.H. Kyabje Zhenphen Rinpoche (Dudjom Tersar lineage)

Day Significane: Even if others, equal or inferior to me in status, Should, out of arrogance, disparage me, To honour them, as I would my teacher, By bowing down my head before them—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

Day Significane: Even though I may be destitute and despised by all, Beset with terrible illness and plagued by evil spirits, Still to take upon myself all beings’ ills and harmful actions, Without ever losing heart—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

Day Significane: Even though I may be famous and revered by all, And as rich as Vaiśravaṇa, the god of wealth himself, To see the futility of all the glory and riches of this world And to remain without conceit—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to subdue the mind With the forces of loving kindness and compassion. For unless the real adversary—my own anger—is defeated, Outer enemies, though I may conquer them, will continue to appear.
Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to turn away immediately From those things which bring desire and attachment. For the pleasures of the senses are just like salty water: The more we taste of them, the more our thirst increases.
Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is never to entertain concepts, Which revolve around dualistic notions of perceiver and perceived, In the knowledge that all these appearances are but the mind itself, Whilst mind’s own nature is forever beyond the limitations of ideas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A day dedicated to healing prayers, longevity practices, and the cultivation of compassion for those who are ill.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane:
Bhagavan, whose compassion is equal toward all,
Just hearing your name clears the suffering of lower realms;
Medicine Buddha, healer of the diseases of the Three Poisons,
To you, the Light of Lapis Lazuli, I prostrate.
Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings grief and sorrow.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to let go of grasping When encountering things one finds pleasant or attractive, Considering them to be like rainbows in the summer skies— Beautiful in appearance, yet in truth devoid of any substance.
Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.
Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A sacred day to honor Guru Padmasambhava, strengthen guru devotion, and renew Vajrayana commitments.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane:
Guru Pema Vajra Tsal
On the tenth day of the horse month, when the malicious ministers of Uḍḍiyāna
Burn you alive, you display the amazing miracle of remaining unscathed,
And, by teaching the Kadü Chökyi Gyatso, lead the king and his entourage to enlightenment—
Padma Vajra Tsal, to you we pray.
 Grant your blessing so that all harm from māras, asuras, vicious earth lords and nāgas is completely eliminated,
And they obey the instructions they are given,
Dharmapālas and guardians carry out all our commands,
And patron gods afford their protection.

1st Event's Details:
Saga Dawa is one of the most significant and blessed festivals in Tibetan Buddhism. Here is a translation of the details you provided:
1. Significance of the Festival
The name ""Saga"" comes from one of the 28 constellations in traditional astrology. On the 15th day of this month, the full moon coincides with the Saga star, hence the name ""Saga Dawa"" (Saga Month). This month is especially sacred as it marks a ""triple-blessed"" occasion: the Buddha's birth, his enlightenment, and his Parinirvana (passing away).
2. Historical Events
* Birth: The anniversary of Buddha Shakyamuni’s birth in the Lumbini Garden. (Note: Some traditions observe the birth on the 7th day of the month).
* Enlightenment: The anniversary of his attainment of Buddhahood at Bodh Gaya at the age of 35.
* Parinirvana: The anniversary of his passing into Nirvana at Kushinagar at the age of 81.
* Timing: While the entire month is holy, the 15th day of the 4th Tibetan month is the most important date.
3. Spiritual Practices and Activities
It is taught that during this month, the merit from any virtuous deed is multiplied one hundred thousand times. Consequently, practitioners diligently engage in the following activities:
* Vegetarianism: Abstaining from meat and following a vegetarian diet for the entire month.
* Life Release: Saving the lives of animals—such as fish or birds—destined for slaughter and setting them free.
* Offerings and Circumambulation: Making offerings at temples and circumambulating (walking around) holy stupas and shrines.
* Almsgiving: Practicing generosity by giving to the poor and needy.

༢། བྱུང་རིམ།
* སྐུ་བལྟམས་པ། སངས་རྒྱས་བཅོམ་ལྡན་འདས་ལུམ་བི་ནིའི་ཚལ་དུ་སྐུ་བལྟམས་པའི་དུས་ཆེན་ཡིན། (འགའ་ཞིག་གིས་ཚེས་ ༧ ཉིན་སྐུ་བལྟམས་པར་བཞེད།)

Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane:
Guru Sengé Dradrok
On the tenth day of the sheep month, in India’s southern reaches,
You grind to dust the tīrthikas, along with their gods and guardians,
And hoist aloft the victory banner of Buddha’s teaching
Mighty Senge Dradok, to you we pray.
Grant your blessing so that all obstacles are pacified, longevity, prosperity and perfect qualities are increased,
We gain control over all perceptions, utterly vanquish all opponents,
We are victorious over the four maras, and actualize the benefits of generosity
Towards all beings in the three worlds.

1st Event: The Holy Anniversary of Guru Rinpoche’s Birthday
1st Event's Details:
Greetings on the Occasion of the Holy Anniversary of Guru Rinpoche’s Birth
The tenth day of the fifth Tibetan month (the first month of the Monkey Year/season) is a special and extraordinary festival marking the birth of the Great Master Padmasambhava, known as Lake-Born Vajra (Tsokyey Dorje), within the lotus ocean.
1. Significance of the Festival
 * The Actual Birth Anniversary: On the tenth day of the Monkey Month, the Great Master emanated as a beam of light from the Western Pure Land of Sukhavati. He was miraculously born within a lotus blossom in the Dhanakosha Lake of Oddiyana.
 * A Time of Great Blessing: It is traditionally believed that any virtuous or meritorious deeds performed on this sacred day are multiplied a hundred thousand times.
2. Recommended Spiritual Practices
In connection with this festival, it is beneficial for all devotees to engage in the following activities:
 * Guru Yoga Meditation: Visualizing Guru Rinpoche above the crown of one's head and receiving his profound blessings.
 * Recitation of the Vajra Guru Mantra: Chanting as many repetitions as possible of the mantra: Om Ah Hum Vajra Guru Padma Siddhi Hum.
 * Offering Feast Puja (Tsok): Performing the Ganachakra (Feast Offering) based on the two stages of the path (Generation and Completion).
 * Reciting Prayers: Reciting the Sampa Lhundrup (Spontaneous Fulfillment of Wishes) and Barchey Lamsel (Clearing Obstacles from the Path).
> A Dedication Prayer:
> Through the blessings of the Second Buddha, the Great Master Padmasambhava, may there be peace in the world, and may the cause of the Tibetan people's religion and politics swiftly find its truthful resolution.

2nd Event's Details:
Universal Smoke Offering  Day (Dzam Ling Spyi Sang)
The Universal Smoke Offering is a significant religious ritual that is widely practiced throughout the Land of Snows (Tibet).
1. Origins
The 15th day of the 5th Tibetan month is the day of Dzam Ling Spyi Sang. This tradition originated after Guru Padmasambhava completed the construction of the Samye Migyur Lhungyi Drupe Tsuglagkhang (Samye Monastery). He subdued the fierce spirits and local deities of Tibet, binding them by oath to protect the Dharma. To celebrate, a grand smoke offering ceremony was performed on the peak of Hepo Ri, marking the beginning of this tradition.
2. Meaning of the Term
 * Dzam Ling (Universal): Refers to all sentient beings living in this world (Jambudvipa).
 * Spyi Sang (General/Collective Offering): Refers to a collective ritual performed by the community to honor the protective deities and offer incense.
3. Benefits
The benefits of performing this smoke offering, both temporary and ultimate, are inconceivable:
 * Pleasing the Protectors: It pleases the local deities (Yul-lha) and guardians of the land (Gzhi-bdag), as well as the Dharma protectors (Dharmapalas), increasing their power to assist.
 * Dispelling Obstacles: It clears away interferences caused by humans and non-humans, and averts negative astrological influences or "bad luck" associated with specific years, months, or days.
 * Boosting Lung-ta (Windhorse): It increases one's life force, merit, and prosperity, enhancing the "Windhorse" (energy) so that activities and projects meet with success.
 * Purifying the Environment: It has the power to purify the external environment and the internal "pollutions" (Drib) or diseases affecting sentient beings.
 * Promoting Peace: Since the focus is on the entire world, it creates the auspicious connection for world peace and the elimination of war and famine.
4. Ritual Substances
When performing the smoke offering, it is vital to offer pure substances, primarily fragrant woods such as:
 * Juniper (Shug-pa)
 * Mugwort (Mkhan-pa)
 * Rhododendron/Dwarf Anthopogon (Ba-lu)
   Additionally, the "Three Whites" (milk, curd, butter), the "Three Sweets" (sugar, honey, molasses), and various medicinal herbs are included.
> Key Point: The essence of the smoke offering is to maintain a pure and sincere motivation, making prayers for the happiness and well-being of all sentient beings.

 * ལྷ་སྲུང་མཉེས་པ། ཡུལ་ལྷ་གཞི་བདག་དང་། ཆོས་སྐྱོང་སྲུང་མ་རྣམས་མཉེས་ཤིང་ནུས་པ་རྒྱས་པར་བྱེད།
 * བར་ཆད་སེལ་བ། མི་དང་མི་མ་ཡིན་པས་བཟོས་པའི་བར་ཆད་དང་། ལོ་ཟླ་ཞག་དུས་ཀྱི་ཀག་སོགས་ངན་པ་ཐམས་ཅད་བཟློག་པར་བྱེད།

Day Significane: Even if others whom I have cared for like children of my own Should turn upon me and treat me as an enemy, To regard them only with special fondness and affection, As a mother would her ailing child—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; very negative coincidence.

Lunar Status: The (Tibetan Day) is doubled this month
Day Significane: Even if others, equal or inferior to me in status, Should, out of arrogance, disparage me, To honour them, as I would my teacher, By bowing down my head before them—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Ambrosia: Strengthens healthy long life; good for business.

Lunar Status: The (Tibetan day) is doubled this month
Day Significane: Even though I may be destitute and despised by all, Beset with terrible illness and plagued by evil spirits, Still to take upon myself all beings’ ills and harmful actions, Without ever losing heart—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings thieves and robbers.

Day Significane: Even though I may be famous and revered by all, And as rich as Vaiśravaṇa, the god of wealth himself, To see the futility of all the glory and riches of this world And to remain without conceit—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Achievement: Fulfils wishes and brings blessings.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to subdue the mind With the forces of loving kindness and compassion. For unless the real adversary—my own anger—is defeated, Outer enemies, though I may conquer them, will continue to appear.
Meaning of Coincidence: Achievement: Fulfils wishes and brings blessings.
Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to turn away immediately From those things which bring desire and attachment. For the pleasures of the senses are just like salty water: The more we taste of them, the more our thirst increases.
Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is never to entertain concepts, Which revolve around dualistic notions of perceiver and perceived, In the knowledge that all these appearances are but the mind itself, Whilst mind’s own nature is forever beyond the limitations of ideas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to let go of grasping When encountering things one finds pleasant or attractive, Considering them to be like rainbows in the summer skies— Beautiful in appearance, yet in truth devoid of any substance.
Meaning of Coincidence: Ample Food/Clothing: Good for giving generously.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to recognize delusion Whenever one is confronted by adversity or misfortune. For these sufferings are just like the death of a child in a dream, And it’s so exhausting to cling to delusory perceptions as real.
Meaning of Coincidence: Ambrosia: Strengthens healthy long life; good for business.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to give out of generosity, With no hopes of karmic recompense or expectation of reward. For if those who seek awakening must give even their own bodies, What need is there to mention mere outer objects and possessions?
Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to observe ethical restraint, Without the slightest intention of continuing in saṃsāric existence. For lacking discipline one will never secure even one’s own well-being, And so any thought of bringing benefit to others would be absurd.
Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings grief and sorrow.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cultivate patience, Free from any trace of animosity towards anyone at all, Since any potential source of harm is like a priceless treasure To the bodhisattva who is eager to enjoy a wealth of virtue.
Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to strive with enthusiastic diligence— The source of all good qualities—when working for the sake of all who live; Seeing that even śrāvakas and pratyekabuddhas, who labour for themselves alone, Exert themselves as if urgently trying to extinguish fires upon their heads.
Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.

བོད་ཀྱི་ལོ་ཐོའི་ནང་ཟླ་རེའི་ཚེས་ ༢༩ ནི་*ཡི་དམ་དང་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་*རྣམས་ཀྱི་དུས་བཟང་དུ་བརྩི་བ་ཡིན། ལྷག་པར་དུ་ལོ་མཇུག་གི་དགུ་གཏོར་དང་། ཟླ་རེའི་ནང་གི་ནག་པོའི་ཕྱོགས་ཀྱི་མཐའ་མར་ཉེ་བའི་སྐབས་འདིར། དྲག་པོའི་ལས་སྒྲུབ་པ་དང་བར་ཆད་བཟློག་པའི་དུས་ཁྱད་པར་ཅན་ཞིག་ཏུ་ངོས་འཛིན་བྱེད།

 * གསོལ་ཁ་འབུལ་བ། ཆོས་སྐྱོང་སྲུང་མ་རྣམས་ལ་གཏོར་མ་དང་། གསེར་སྐྱེམས། བསངས་མཆོད་སོགས་རྒྱ་ཆེན་པོ་འབུལ་བ།

༣། ཕན་ཡོན།
ཚེས་ ༢༩ ལ་སྲུང་མ་མཆོད་པའི་ཕན་ཡོན་ནི་བསམ་གྱིས་མི་ཁྱབ་པ་ཡོད་དེ།

 * དམ་ཚིག་གཙང་མར་བཟོ་བ། བླ་མ་དང་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་དབར་གྱི་འགལ་འཁྲུལ་དང་དམ་ཚིག་ཉམས་པ་རྣམས་སོར་ཆུད་པར་བྱེད།

 * བསྟན་པ་སྲུང་བ། སྒེར་གྱི་དོན་ཙམ་མ་ཡིན་པར་སངས་རྒྱས་ཀྱི་བསྟན་པ་དང་འཛམ་གླིང་ཞི་བདེ་སྲུང་བའི་ནུས་པ་སྤེལ།
༤། དམིགས་བསལ་གྱི་དོན་སྙིང་།
སྲུང་མ་མཆོད་པ་ནི་ལྷ་གསོལ་བ་ཙམ་མ་ཡིན་པར། རང་གི་སེམས་ནང་གི་*དྲག་པོའི་ནུས་པ་*འཕེལ་བར་བྱས་ཏེ། ནང་གི་ཉོན་མོངས་པའི་དགྲ་བཅོམ་པ་དང་། ཕྱིའི་འཇིག་རྟེན་གྱི་བྱ་བ་གང་ཡང་བརྟན་པོར་སྐྱོང་བའི་སྙིང་སྟོབས་བསྐྱེད་པ་ལ་གོ་དགོས།
> ལམ་སྟོན། ཉིན་འདིར་ཁྱེད་ཀྱིས་རང་གི་ཡི་དམ་མམ་སྲུང་མའི་མ་ཎི་དང་སྙིང་པོ་བཟླས་པ་དང་། གཙང་བྲ་ལ་གཟབ་ནས་གསེར་སྐྱེམས་འབུལ་ན་ཤིན་ཏུ་ལེགས།"

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A day of introspection and renewal, traditionally used for purification practices and restoring commitments.

Day Significane:
"The Day of the Hundred Sacred Peaceful and Wrathful Deities"

Confession of Negative Actions (Digpa Shagpa): As it is the final day of the month, it is a day for purifying and confessing the non-virtuous actions committed throughout that month.
Restoration and Purification (Sojong): It is also the day when the monastic community (Sangha) gathers to perform the "Sojong" ritual, a ceremony for restoring vows and purifying transgressions according to the Vinaya (monastic discipline).
Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cultivate concentration, Which utterly transcends the four formless absorptions, In the knowledge that mental afflictions are overcome entirely Through penetrating insight suffused with stable calm.
Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cultivate wisdom, Beyond the three conceptual spheres, alongside skilful means, Since it is not possible to attain the perfect level of awakening Through the other five pāramitās alone, in wisdom’s absence.
Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to study, reflect and meditate, Tirelessly, both day and night, without ever straying into idleness, In order to free oneself and others from this ocean of saṃsāra, Having gained this supreme vessel—a free, well-favoured human life, so difficult to find.
Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to leave behind one’s homeland, Where our attachment to family and friends overwhelms us like a torrent, While our aversion towards enemies rages inside us like a blazing fire, And delusion’s darkness obscures what must be adopted and abandoned.
Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.
1st Event: Chokhor Duchen (The Festival of Turning the Wheel of Dharma)
1st Event's Details:
The Significance of the Festival
The name "Chokhor Duchen" refers to the "Festival of Turning the Wheel of Dharma." This is the day marking the very first time Buddha Shakyamuni turned the Wheel of Dharma (gave his first teachings).
Historical Origin
After Buddha Shakyamuni attained complete enlightenment at Bodh Gaya, India, he remained silent without teaching for seven weeks. Following this, at the request of the gods Brahma and Indra, he traveled to the Deer Park in Varanasi on the 4th day of the 6th Tibetan month. There, he first turned the "Wheel of Dharma of the Four Noble Truths" for his five first disciples and others.
1st Event's Details (Tibetan):
དུས་ཆེན་གྱི་དོན་སྙིང་།
"ཆོས་འཁོར་དུས་ཆེན་" ཞེས་པ་ནི་ "ཆོས་ཀྱི་འཁོར་ལོ་བསྐོར་བའི་དུས་ཆེན་" ལ་གོ་བ་ཡིན། འདི་ནི་སྟོན་པ་སངས་རྒྱས་བཅོམ་ལྡན་འདས་ཀྱིས་ཐོག་མར་ཆོས་ཀྱི་འཁོར་ལོ་བསྐོར་བའི་ཉིན་མོ་དེ་ཡིན།
བྱུང་རིམ།
སྟོན་པ་སངས་རྒྱས་བཅོམ་ལྡན་འདས་ཀྱིས་རྒྱ་གར་རྡོ་རྗེ་གདན་དུ་མངོན་པར་རྫོགས་པར་སངས་རྒྱས་ནས་བདུན་ཕྲག་བདུན་གྱི་རིང་ལ་ཆོས་མ་གསུངས་པར་བཞུགས། དེའི་རྗེས་ལྷ་ཚངས་པ་དང་བརྒྱ་བྱིན་གྱིས་གསོལ་བ་བཏབ་པ་ལྟར། བོད་ཟླ་ ༦ ཚེས་ ༤ ཉིན་ཝཱ་རཱ་ཎ་སཱིའི་རི་དྭགས་ནགས་ཚལ་དུ་འཁོར་ལྔ་སྡེ་བཟང་པོ་སོགས་ལ་ "བདེན་བཞིའི་ཆོས་འཁོར་" ཐོག་མར་བསྐོར་བའི་དུས་ཆེན་ཞིག་ཡིན།

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to take to solitary places, Avoiding the unwholesome, so that destructive emotions gradually fade away, And, in the absence of distraction, virtuous practice naturally gains strength; Whilst, with awareness clearly focused, we gain conviction in the teachings.
Meaning of Coincidence: Ample Food/Clothing: Good for giving generously.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to renounce this life’s concerns, For friends and relatives, long acquainted, must all go their separate ways; Wealth and prized possessions, painstakingly acquired, must all be left behind; And consciousness, the guest who lodges in the body, must in time depart.
Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to avoid destructive friends, In whose company the three poisons of the mind grow stronger, And we engage less and less in study, reflection and meditation, So that love and compassion fade away until they are no more.
Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

Day Significane:
Bhagavan, whose compassion is equal toward all,
Just hearing your name clears the suffering of lower realms;
Medicine Buddha, healer of the diseases of the Three Poisons,
To you, the Light of Lapis Lazuli, I prostrate.
Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.
1st Event: The first half-yearly examination of the Shedra (Monastic College) and School of Odisha Dudjom Vihara Monastery.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cherish spiritual friends, By regarding them as even more precious than one’s own body, Since they are the ones who will help to rid us of all our faults, And make our virtues grow ever greater just like the waxing moon.
Meaning of Coincidence: Certainty: Fulfills wishes speedily; resolves disputes.
Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A sacred day to honor Guru Padmasambhava, strengthen guru devotion, and renew Vajrayana commitments.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane:
Guru Khanding Tsal
On the tenth day of the bird month, the tīrthikas of Zangling,
Fling you into the river Ganges, but you dance in the sky, and reverse the current.
When the whole land is in peril, and they beg you for refuge and protection, you bring them all within the Dharma
Guru Khanding Tsal, to you we pray.
Grant your blessing so that all chronic and immune system diseases are healed, our helpers grow in number, and our wishes are fulfilled,
Food and resources of every kind increase, as well as everything we need,
Our mind is peaceful, our speech blazes with power,
And our samādhi meditation becomes sublime and supreme.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to take refuge in the Three Jewels, Since they will never fail to provide protection for all who call upon them, For whom are the ordinary gods of this world ever capable of helping, As long as they themselves are trapped within saṃsāra’s vicious cycle?

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is never to commit a harmful act, Even though not to do so might put one’s very life at risk, For the Sage himself has taught how negative actions will ripen Into the manifold miseries of the lower realms, so difficult to endure.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to strive towards the goal, Which is the supreme state of changeless, everlasting liberation, Since all the happiness of the three realms lasts but a moment, And then is quickly gone, just like dewdrops on blades of grass.
Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to arouse bodhicitta, So as to bring freedom to all sentient beings, infinite in number. For how can true happiness ever be found while our mothers, Who have cared for us throughout the ages, endure such pain?
Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; very negative coincidence.
