Day Significane: "The Day of Amitabha Buddha"
Meaning of Coincidence: Ambrosia: Strengthens healthy long life; good for business.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to make a genuine exchange Of one’s own happiness and well-being for all the sufferings of others. Since all misery comes from seeking happiness for oneself alone, Whilst perfect buddhahood is born from the wish for others’ good.
Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings thieves and robbers.

Day Significane: Even if others, in the grips of great desire, should steal, Or encourage others to take away, all the wealth that I possess, To dedicate to them entirely my body, possessions and all my merits From the past, present and future— this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Achievement: Fulfils wishes and brings blessings.

Day Significane: Even if others should seek to cut off my head, Though I’ve done them not the slightest wrong, To take upon myself, out of compassion, All the harms they have amassed—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

Day Significane: Even if others should declare before the world All manner of unpleasant things about me, To speak only of their qualities in return, With a mind that’s filled with love—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.
Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

Day Significane: Even if others should expose my hidden faults or deride me When speaking amidst great gatherings of many people, To conceive of them as spiritual friends and to bow Before them in respect—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.

Day Significane: Even if others whom I have cared for like children of my own Should turn upon me and treat me as an enemy, To regard them only with special fondness and affection, As a mother would her ailing child—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; very negative coincidence.

Day Significane: Even if others, equal or inferior to me in status, Should, out of arrogance, disparage me, To honour them, as I would my teacher, By bowing down my head before them—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Ambrosia: Strengthens healthy long life; good for business.

Day Significane: Even though I may be destitute and despised by all, Beset with terrible illness and plagued by evil spirits, Still to take upon myself all beings’ ills and harmful actions, Without ever losing heart—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Certainty: Fulfills wishes speedily; resolves disputes.

Day Significane: Even though I may be famous and revered by all, And as rich as Vaiśravaṇa, the god of wealth himself, To see the futility of all the glory and riches of this world And to remain without conceit—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A day connected to wisdom energy and enlightened activity — suitable for mantra recitation and inner practice.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane:
"The Day of the Dakini Festival"                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          Mother of all the victorious ones, Vajrayoginī,
Mother of diversity, Prajñāpāramitā,
Padma's consort, joyful, passionate and smiling—
Yeshe Tsogyal, homage to you!
Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to subdue the mind With the forces of loving kindness and compassion. For unless the real adversary—my own anger—is defeated, Outer enemies, though I may conquer them, will continue to appear.
Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.
1st Event: Parinirvana of Nyoshul Khen Rinpoche (1932–1999)

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to turn away immediately From those things which bring desire and attachment. For the pleasures of the senses are just like salty water: The more we taste of them, the more our thirst increases.
Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A day to make offerings and prayers to Dharma Protectors for the removal of obstacles and protection of practice.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane: Day dedicated to invoking Dharma Protectors like Mahakala and Palden Lhamo to remove obstacles and strengthen protection.
Unnamed: 25:
"བོད་ཟླ་རེའི་ཚེས་ ༢༩ ཉིན་ནི་*ཆོས་སྐྱོང་སྲུང་མའི་དུས་ཆེན་ནམ་དུས་བཟང་*གལ་ཆེན་ཞིག་ཡིན། 
༡། གོ་དོན།
བོད་ཀྱི་ལོ་ཐོའི་ནང་ཟླ་རེའི་ཚེས་ ༢༩ ནི་*ཡི་དམ་དང་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་*རྣམས་ཀྱི་དུས་བཟང་དུ་བརྩི་བ་ཡིན། ལྷག་པར་དུ་ལོ་མཇུག་གི་དགུ་གཏོར་དང་། ཟླ་རེའི་ནང་གི་ནག་པོའི་ཕྱོགས་ཀྱི་མཐའ་མར་ཉེ་བའི་སྐབས་འདིར། དྲག་པོའི་ལས་སྒྲུབ་པ་དང་བར་ཆད་བཟློག་པའི་དུས་ཁྱད་པར་ཅན་ཞིག་ཏུ་ངོས་འཛིན་བྱེད།
༢། བྱེད་སྒོ།
ཉིན་འདིར་དགོན་སྡེ་ཁག་དང་ཁྱིམ་ཚང་སོ་སོའི་ནང་དུ།
 * གསོལ་ཁ་འབུལ་བ། ཆོས་སྐྱོང་སྲུང་མ་རྣམས་ལ་གཏོར་མ་དང་། གསེར་སྐྱེམས། བསངས་མཆོད་སོགས་རྒྱ་ཆེན་པོ་འབུལ་བ།
 * བསྐང་བཤགས་གཏོང་བ། རང་གི་དམ་ཚིག་ཉམས་ཆག་རྣམས་བཤགས་ཤིང་། སྲུང་མའི་ཐུགས་དམ་བསྐང་བའི་ཆོ་ག་བྱེད་པ།
༣། ཕན་ཡོན།
ཚེས་ ༢༩ ལ་སྲུང་མ་མཆོད་པའི་ཕན་ཡོན་ནི་བསམ་གྱིས་མི་ཁྱབ་པ་ཡོད་དེ།
 * བར་ཆད་ཞི་བ། ལམ་ལྷོངས་མ་ཡོང་བའི་གེགས་བར་དང་། མི་ཁ་ཁ་མཆུ། གནོད་པ་ཐམས་ཅད་ཞི་བར་བྱེད།
 * དམ་ཚིག་གཙང་མར་བཟོ་བ། བླ་མ་དང་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་དབར་གྱི་འགལ་འཁྲུལ་དང་དམ་ཚིག་ཉམས་པ་རྣམས་སོར་ཆུད་པར་བྱེད།
 * བྱ་བ་ལམ་འགྲོ། ཆོས་བཞིན་སྒྲུབ་པའི་ལས་དོན་ཐམས་ཅད་ལ་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་གིས་མགོན་སྐྱོབ་དང་ཕྱྭ་གཡང་སྩོལ་བར་བྱེད།
 * བསྟན་པ་སྲུང་བ། སྒེར་གྱི་དོན་ཙམ་མ་ཡིན་པར་སངས་རྒྱས་ཀྱི་བསྟན་པ་དང་འཛམ་གླིང་ཞི་བདེ་སྲུང་བའི་ནུས་པ་སྤེལ།
༤། དམིགས་བསལ་གྱི་དོན་སྙིང་།
སྲུང་མ་མཆོད་པ་ནི་ལྷ་གསོལ་བ་ཙམ་མ་ཡིན་པར། རང་གི་སེམས་ནང་གི་*དྲག་པོའི་ནུས་པ་*འཕེལ་བར་བྱས་ཏེ། ནང་གི་ཉོན་མོངས་པའི་དགྲ་བཅོམ་པ་དང་། ཕྱིའི་འཇིག་རྟེན་གྱི་བྱ་བ་གང་ཡང་བརྟན་པོར་སྐྱོང་བའི་སྙིང་སྟོབས་བསྐྱེད་པ་ལ་གོ་དགོས།
> ལམ་སྟོན། ཉིན་འདིར་ཁྱེད་ཀྱིས་རང་གི་ཡི་དམ་མམ་སྲུང་མའི་མ་ཎི་དང་སྙིང་པོ་བཟླས་པ་དང་། གཙང་བྲ་ལ་གཟབ་ནས་གསེར་སྐྱེམས་འབུལ་ན་ཤིན་ཏུ་ལེགས།"
Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.
Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A day of introspection and renewal, traditionally used for purification practices and restoring commitments.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane:
"The Day of the Hundred Sacred Peaceful and Wrathful Deities"

Confession of Negative Actions (Digpa Shagpa): As it is the final day of the month, it is a day for purifying and confessing the non-virtuous actions committed throughout that month.
Restoration and Purification (Sojong): It is also the day when the monastic community (Sangha) gathers to perform the "Sojong" ritual, a ceremony for restoring vows and purifying transgressions according to the Vinaya (monastic discipline).
Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is never to entertain concepts, Which revolve around dualistic notions of perceiver and perceived, In the knowledge that all these appearances are but the mind itself, Whilst mind’s own nature is forever beyond the limitations of ideas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to let go of grasping When encountering things one finds pleasant or attractive, Considering them to be like rainbows in the summer skies— Beautiful in appearance, yet in truth devoid of any substance.
Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to recognize delusion Whenever one is confronted by adversity or misfortune. For these sufferings are just like the death of a child in a dream, And it’s so exhausting to cling to delusory perceptions as real.
Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to give out of generosity, With no hopes of karmic recompense or expectation of reward. For if those who seek awakening must give even their own bodies, What need is there to mention mere outer objects and possessions?
Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to observe ethical restraint, Without the slightest intention of continuing in saṃsāric existence. For lacking discipline one will never secure even one’s own well-being, And so any thought of bringing benefit to others would be absurd.
Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.
1st Event: Parinirvana of H.H. Kybaje Trulshi Rinpoche

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cultivate patience, Free from any trace of animosity towards anyone at all, Since any potential source of harm is like a priceless treasure To the bodhisattva who is eager to enjoy a wealth of virtue.
Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to strive with enthusiastic diligence— The source of all good qualities—when working for the sake of all who live; Seeing that even śrāvakas and pratyekabuddhas, who labour for themselves alone, Exert themselves as if urgently trying to extinguish fires upon their heads.
Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A day dedicated to healing prayers, longevity practices, and the cultivation of compassion for those who are ill.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane:
Bhagavan, whose compassion is equal toward all,
Just hearing your name clears the suffering of lower realms;
Medicine Buddha, healer of the diseases of the Three Poisons,
To you, the Light of Lapis Lazuli, I prostrate.
Meaning of Coincidence: Certainty: Fulfills wishes speedily; resolves disputes.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cultivate concentration, Which utterly transcends the four formless absorptions, In the knowledge that mental afflictions are overcome entirely Through penetrating insight suffused with stable calm.
Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.
Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A sacred day to honor Guru Padmasambhava, strengthen guru devotion, and renew Vajrayana commitments.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane:
Guru Dechen Nyima Özer
On the tenth day of the dog month, the tīrthikas give you poison,
Unharmed, you transform it into nectar, and shine in splendour.
They and all their followers are inspired with faith and enter the teaching of Buddha
Guru of Great Bliss, Nyima Özer, to you we pray.
 Grant your blessing so that obstacles of the year, month, day and time as well as ill omens are pacified,
All obstacles in the way of the Dharma and liberation are dispelled,
Any decline in our personal power and lungta is repaired
And we are victorious in every direction.
Unnamed: 25:
ཁྱི་ཟླའི་ཚེས་བཅུར་མུ་སྟེགས་དུག་སྦྱོར་གྱིས། །
མ་ཚུགས་བདུད་རྩིར་བསྒྱུར་ནས་གཟི་མདངས་འབར། །
འཁོར་བཅས་དད་ཐོབ་ནང་གི་བསྟན་ལ་བཅུག །
བདེ་ཆེན་ཉི་མ་འོད་ཟེར་ལ་གསོལ་བ་འདེབས། །
ལོ་ཟླ་ཞག་དུས་ཀེག་དང་ལྟས་ངན་ཞི། །
ཆོས་དང་ཐར་པའི་བར་དུ་གཅོད་པ་སེལ། །
དབང་ཐང་ཀླུང་རྟ་གུད་སོགས་སོར་ཆུད་ཅིང༌། །
ཕྱོགས་ལས་རྣམ་པར་རྒྱལ་བར་བྱིན་གྱིས་རློབས། །
Meaning of Coincidence: Achievement: Fulfils wishes and brings blessings.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cultivate wisdom, Beyond the three conceptual spheres, alongside skilful means, Since it is not possible to attain the perfect level of awakening Through the other five pāramitās alone, in wisdom’s absence.
Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to study, reflect and meditate, Tirelessly, both day and night, without ever straying into idleness, In order to free oneself and others from this ocean of saṃsāra, Having gained this supreme vessel—a free, well-favoured human life, so difficult to find.
Meaning of Coincidence: Ambrosia: Strengthens healthy long life; good for business.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to leave behind one’s homeland, Where our attachment to family and friends overwhelms us like a torrent, While our aversion towards enemies rages inside us like a blazing fire, And delusion’s darkness obscures what must be adopted and abandoned.
Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; very negative coincidence.

Lunar Status: The (Tibetan Day) is doubled this month
Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to take to solitary places, Avoiding the unwholesome, so that destructive emotions gradually fade away, And, in the absence of distraction, virtuous practice naturally gains strength; Whilst, with awareness clearly focused, we gain conviction in the teachings.
Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

Lunar Status: The (Tibetan day) is doubled this month
Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to renounce this life’s concerns, For friends and relatives, long acquainted, must all go their separate ways; Wealth and prized possessions, painstakingly acquired, must all be left behind; And consciousness, the guest who lodges in the body, must in time depart.
Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings thieves and robbers.

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A powerful day for merit-making, purification, offerings, and strengthening positive intentions.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane: "The Day of Amitabha Buddha"
Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to avoid destructive friends, In whose company the three poisons of the mind grow stronger, And we engage less and less in study, reflection and meditation, So that love and compassion fade away until they are no more.
Meaning of Coincidence: Achievement: Fulfils wishes and brings blessings.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cherish spiritual friends, By regarding them as even more precious than one’s own body, Since they are the ones who will help to rid us of all our faults, And make our virtues grow ever greater just like the waxing moon.
Meaning of Coincidence: Ample Food/Clothing: Good for giving generously.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to take refuge in the Three Jewels, Since they will never fail to provide protection for all who call upon them, For whom are the ordinary gods of this world ever capable of helping, As long as they themselves are trapped within saṃsāra’s vicious cycle?
Meaning of Coincidence: Ambrosia: Strengthens healthy long life; good for business.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is never to commit a harmful act, Even though not to do so might put one’s very life at risk, For the Sage himself has taught how negative actions will ripen Into the manifold miseries of the lower realms, so difficult to endure.
Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.
Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to strive towards the goal, Which is the supreme state of changeless, everlasting liberation, Since all the happiness of the three realms lasts but a moment, And then is quickly gone, just like dewdrops on blades of grass.
Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to arouse bodhicitta, So as to bring freedom to all sentient beings, infinite in number. For how can true happiness ever be found while our mothers, Who have cared for us throughout the ages, endure such pain?
Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to make a genuine exchange Of one’s own happiness and well-being for all the sufferings of others. Since all misery comes from seeking happiness for oneself alone, Whilst perfect buddhahood is born from the wish for others’ good.
Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.

Day Significane: Even if others, in the grips of great desire, should steal, Or encourage others to take away, all the wealth that I possess, To dedicate to them entirely my body, possessions and all my merits From the past, present and future— this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Achievement: Fulfils wishes and brings blessings.

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A day connected to wisdom energy and enlightened activity — suitable for mantra recitation and inner practice.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane:
"The Day of the Dakini Festival"                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          Mother of all the victorious ones, Vajrayoginī,
Mother of diversity, Prajñāpāramitā,
Padma's consort, joyful, passionate and smiling—
Yeshe Tsogyal, homage to you!
Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

Day Significane: Even if others should seek to cut off my head, Though I’ve done them not the slightest wrong, To take upon myself, out of compassion, All the harms they have amassed—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Ambrosia: Strengthens healthy long life; good for business.

Day Significane: Even if others should declare before the world All manner of unpleasant things about me, To speak only of their qualities in return, With a mind that’s filled with love—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

Day Significane: Even if others should expose my hidden faults or deride me When speaking amidst great gatherings of many people, To conceive of them as spiritual friends and to bow Before them in respect—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A day to make offerings and prayers to Dharma Protectors for the removal of obstacles and protection of practice.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane: Day dedicated to invoking Dharma Protectors like Mahakala and Palden Lhamo to remove obstacles and strengthen protection.
Unnamed: 25:
"བོད་ཟླ་རེའི་ཚེས་ ༢༩ ཉིན་ནི་*ཆོས་སྐྱོང་སྲུང་མའི་དུས་ཆེན་ནམ་དུས་བཟང་*གལ་ཆེན་ཞིག་ཡིན། 
༡། གོ་དོན།
བོད་ཀྱི་ལོ་ཐོའི་ནང་ཟླ་རེའི་ཚེས་ ༢༩ ནི་*ཡི་དམ་དང་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་*རྣམས་ཀྱི་དུས་བཟང་དུ་བརྩི་བ་ཡིན། ལྷག་པར་དུ་ལོ་མཇུག་གི་དགུ་གཏོར་དང་། ཟླ་རེའི་ནང་གི་ནག་པོའི་ཕྱོགས་ཀྱི་མཐའ་མར་ཉེ་བའི་སྐབས་འདིར། དྲག་པོའི་ལས་སྒྲུབ་པ་དང་བར་ཆད་བཟློག་པའི་དུས་ཁྱད་པར་ཅན་ཞིག་ཏུ་ངོས་འཛིན་བྱེད།
༢། བྱེད་སྒོ།
ཉིན་འདིར་དགོན་སྡེ་ཁག་དང་ཁྱིམ་ཚང་སོ་སོའི་ནང་དུ།
 * གསོལ་ཁ་འབུལ་བ། ཆོས་སྐྱོང་སྲུང་མ་རྣམས་ལ་གཏོར་མ་དང་། གསེར་སྐྱེམས། བསངས་མཆོད་སོགས་རྒྱ་ཆེན་པོ་འབུལ་བ།
 * བསྐང་བཤགས་གཏོང་བ། རང་གི་དམ་ཚིག་ཉམས་ཆག་རྣམས་བཤགས་ཤིང་། སྲུང་མའི་ཐུགས་དམ་བསྐང་བའི་ཆོ་ག་བྱེད་པ།
༣། ཕན་ཡོན།
ཚེས་ ༢༩ ལ་སྲུང་མ་མཆོད་པའི་ཕན་ཡོན་ནི་བསམ་གྱིས་མི་ཁྱབ་པ་ཡོད་དེ།
 * བར་ཆད་ཞི་བ། ལམ་ལྷོངས་མ་ཡོང་བའི་གེགས་བར་དང་། མི་ཁ་ཁ་མཆུ། གནོད་པ་ཐམས་ཅད་ཞི་བར་བྱེད།
 * དམ་ཚིག་གཙང་མར་བཟོ་བ། བླ་མ་དང་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་དབར་གྱི་འགལ་འཁྲུལ་དང་དམ་ཚིག་ཉམས་པ་རྣམས་སོར་ཆུད་པར་བྱེད།
 * བྱ་བ་ལམ་འགྲོ། ཆོས་བཞིན་སྒྲུབ་པའི་ལས་དོན་ཐམས་ཅད་ལ་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་གིས་མགོན་སྐྱོབ་དང་ཕྱྭ་གཡང་སྩོལ་བར་བྱེད།
 * བསྟན་པ་སྲུང་བ། སྒེར་གྱི་དོན་ཙམ་མ་ཡིན་པར་སངས་རྒྱས་ཀྱི་བསྟན་པ་དང་འཛམ་གླིང་ཞི་བདེ་སྲུང་བའི་ནུས་པ་སྤེལ།
༤། དམིགས་བསལ་གྱི་དོན་སྙིང་།
སྲུང་མ་མཆོད་པ་ནི་ལྷ་གསོལ་བ་ཙམ་མ་ཡིན་པར། རང་གི་སེམས་ནང་གི་*དྲག་པོའི་ནུས་པ་*འཕེལ་བར་བྱས་ཏེ། ནང་གི་ཉོན་མོངས་པའི་དགྲ་བཅོམ་པ་དང་། ཕྱིའི་འཇིག་རྟེན་གྱི་བྱ་བ་གང་ཡང་བརྟན་པོར་སྐྱོང་བའི་སྙིང་སྟོབས་བསྐྱེད་པ་ལ་གོ་དགོས།
> ལམ་སྟོན། ཉིན་འདིར་ཁྱེད་ཀྱིས་རང་གི་ཡི་དམ་མམ་སྲུང་མའི་མ་ཎི་དང་སྙིང་པོ་བཟླས་པ་དང་། གཙང་བྲ་ལ་གཟབ་ནས་གསེར་སྐྱེམས་འབུལ་ན་ཤིན་ཏུ་ལེགས།"
Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.
Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A day of introspection and renewal, traditionally used for purification practices and restoring commitments.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane:
"The Day of the Hundred Sacred Peaceful and Wrathful Deities"

Confession of Negative Actions (Digpa Shagpa): As it is the final day of the month, it is a day for purifying and confessing the non-virtuous actions committed throughout that month.
Restoration and Purification (Sojong): It is also the day when the monastic community (Sangha) gathers to perform the "Sojong" ritual, a ceremony for restoring vows and purifying transgressions according to the Vinaya (monastic discipline).
Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.

Day Significane: Even if others whom I have cared for like children of my own Should turn upon me and treat me as an enemy, To regard them only with special fondness and affection, As a mother would her ailing child—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.

Day Significane: Even if others, equal or inferior to me in status, Should, out of arrogance, disparage me, To honour them, as I would my teacher, By bowing down my head before them—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

Day Significane: Even though I may be destitute and despised by all, Beset with terrible illness and plagued by evil spirits, Still to take upon myself all beings’ ills and harmful actions, Without ever losing heart—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.

Day Significane: Even though I may be famous and revered by all, And as rich as Vaiśravaṇa, the god of wealth himself, To see the futility of all the glory and riches of this world And to remain without conceit—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to subdue the mind With the forces of loving kindness and compassion. For unless the real adversary—my own anger—is defeated, Outer enemies, though I may conquer them, will continue to appear.
Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to turn away immediately From those things which bring desire and attachment. For the pleasures of the senses are just like salty water: The more we taste of them, the more our thirst increases.
Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is never to entertain concepts, Which revolve around dualistic notions of perceiver and perceived, In the knowledge that all these appearances are but the mind itself, Whilst mind’s own nature is forever beyond the limitations of ideas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Achievement: Fulfils wishes and brings blessings.
1st Event: Birthday of Anagarika Dharmapala (1864–1933)

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A day dedicated to healing prayers, longevity practices, and the cultivation of compassion for those who are ill.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane:
Bhagavan, whose compassion is equal toward all,
Just hearing your name clears the suffering of lower realms;
Medicine Buddha, healer of the diseases of the Three Poisons,
To you, the Light of Lapis Lazuli, I prostrate.
Meaning of Coincidence: Achievement: Fulfils wishes and brings blessings.
1st Event: The Second half-yearly examination of the Shedra (Monastic College) and School of Odisha Dudjom Vihara Monastery

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to let go of grasping When encountering things one finds pleasant or attractive, Considering them to be like rainbows in the summer skies— Beautiful in appearance, yet in truth devoid of any substance.
Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; very negative coincidence.
Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A sacred day to honor Guru Padmasambhava, strengthen guru devotion, and renew Vajrayana commitments.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane:
Guru Dorjé Tötreng Tsal
On the tenth day of the pig month, the gods and demons of Nepal
Are subjugated by Vajrakīla, and then through accomplishing Yangdak
You attain the vidyādhara stage of supreme mahāmudrā
Dorje Tötreng Tsal, to you we pray.
 Grant your blessing so that all illnesses and harm caused by yamas, mamos and jungpos,
Sudden accidents, and untimely death all are eliminated,
We are freed from dangerous curses, gods and demons obey whatever their commands,
And our own bodies become indestructible as vajra.
Unnamed: 25:
ཕག་ཟླའི་ཚེས་བཅུར་བལ་ཡུལ་ལྷ་འདྲེ་རྣམས། །
ཕུར་བུས་བཏུལ་ནས་ཡང་དག་སྒྲུབ་པ་མཛད། །
ཕྱག་རྒྱ་ཆེན་པོ་མཆོག་གི་རིག་འཛིན་གྲུབ། །
རྡོ་རྗེ་ཐོད་ཕྲེང་རྩལ་ལ་གསོལ་བ་འདེབས། །
གཤིན་རྗེ་མ་མོ་འབྱུང་པོའི་ནད་དང་གདོན། །
གློ་བུར་ཡེ་འདྲོག་དུས་མིན་འཆི་བ་ཞི། །
གཏད་སེལ་ལས་གྲོལ་ལྷ་འདྲེས་ཅི་བཅོལ་ཉན། །
རང་ལུས་རྡོ་རྗེར་འགྱུར་བར་བྱིན་གྱིས་རློབས། །
Meaning of Coincidence: Ambrosia: Strengthens healthy long life; good for business.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to recognize delusion Whenever one is confronted by adversity or misfortune. For these sufferings are just like the death of a child in a dream, And it’s so exhausting to cling to delusory perceptions as real.
Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to give out of generosity, With no hopes of karmic recompense or expectation of reward. For if those who seek awakening must give even their own bodies, What need is there to mention mere outer objects and possessions?
Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to observe ethical restraint, Without the slightest intention of continuing in saṃsāric existence. For lacking discipline one will never secure even one’s own well-being, And so any thought of bringing benefit to others would be absurd.
Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings thieves and robbers.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cultivate patience, Free from any trace of animosity towards anyone at all, Since any potential source of harm is like a priceless treasure To the bodhisattva who is eager to enjoy a wealth of virtue.
Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.
1st Event: Parinirvana of H.H. Kyabje Yangthang Rinpoche (Dorje Drak lineage, 1930–2016)

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A powerful day for merit-making, purification, offerings, and strengthening positive intentions.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane: "The Day of Amitabha Buddha"
Meaning of Coincidence: Achievement: Fulfils wishes and brings blessings.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to strive with enthusiastic diligence— The source of all good qualities—when working for the sake of all who live; Seeing that even śrāvakas and pratyekabuddhas, who labour for themselves alone, Exert themselves as if urgently trying to extinguish fires upon their heads.
Meaning of Coincidence: Ample Food/Clothing: Good for giving generously.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cultivate concentration, Which utterly transcends the four formless absorptions, In the knowledge that mental afflictions are overcome entirely Through penetrating insight suffused with stable calm.
Meaning of Coincidence: Ambrosia: Strengthens healthy long life; good for business.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cultivate wisdom, Beyond the three conceptual spheres, alongside skilful means, Since it is not possible to attain the perfect level of awakening Through the other five pāramitās alone, in wisdom’s absence.
Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; very negative coincidence.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to study, reflect and meditate, Tirelessly, both day and night, without ever straying into idleness, In order to free oneself and others from this ocean of saṃsāra, Having gained this supreme vessel—a free, well-favoured human life, so difficult to find.
Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.
Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts
1st Event: Parinirvana of H.H. Kyabje Dilgo Khyentse Rinpoche (Shechen lineage, 1910–1991)

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to leave behind one’s homeland, Where our attachment to family and friends overwhelms us like a torrent, While our aversion towards enemies rages inside us like a blazing fire, And delusion’s darkness obscures what must be adopted and abandoned.
Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to take to solitary places, Avoiding the unwholesome, so that destructive emotions gradually fade away, And, in the absence of distraction, virtuous practice naturally gains strength; Whilst, with awareness clearly focused, we gain conviction in the teachings.
Meaning of Coincidence: Achievement: Fulfils wishes and brings blessings.
1st Event: Mahatma Gandhi's Birthday (1869–1948)

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to renounce this life’s concerns, For friends and relatives, long acquainted, must all go their separate ways; Wealth and prized possessions, painstakingly acquired, must all be left behind; And consciousness, the guest who lodges in the body, must in time depart.
Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings grief and sorrow.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to avoid destructive friends, In whose company the three poisons of the mind grow stronger, And we engage less and less in study, reflection and meditation, So that love and compassion fade away until they are no more.
Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A day connected to wisdom energy and enlightened activity — suitable for mantra recitation and inner practice.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane:
"The Day of the Dakini Festival"                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          Mother of all the victorious ones, Vajrayoginī,
Mother of diversity, Prajñāpāramitā,
Padma's consort, joyful, passionate and smiling—
Yeshe Tsogyal, homage to you!
Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cherish spiritual friends, By regarding them as even more precious than one’s own body, Since they are the ones who will help to rid us of all our faults, And make our virtues grow ever greater just like the waxing moon.
Meaning of Coincidence: Ample Food/Clothing: Good for giving generously.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to take refuge in the Three Jewels, Since they will never fail to provide protection for all who call upon them, For whom are the ordinary gods of this world ever capable of helping, As long as they themselves are trapped within saṃsāra’s vicious cycle?
Meaning of Coincidence: Ambrosia: Strengthens healthy long life; good for business.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is never to commit a harmful act, Even though not to do so might put one’s very life at risk, For the Sage himself has taught how negative actions will ripen Into the manifold miseries of the lower realms, so difficult to endure.
Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A day to make offerings and prayers to Dharma Protectors for the removal of obstacles and protection of practice.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane: Day dedicated to invoking Dharma Protectors like Mahakala and Palden Lhamo to remove obstacles and strengthen protection.
Unnamed: 25:
"བོད་ཟླ་རེའི་ཚེས་ ༢༩ ཉིན་ནི་*ཆོས་སྐྱོང་སྲུང་མའི་དུས་ཆེན་ནམ་དུས་བཟང་*གལ་ཆེན་ཞིག་ཡིན། 
༡། གོ་དོན།
བོད་ཀྱི་ལོ་ཐོའི་ནང་ཟླ་རེའི་ཚེས་ ༢༩ ནི་*ཡི་དམ་དང་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་*རྣམས་ཀྱི་དུས་བཟང་དུ་བརྩི་བ་ཡིན། ལྷག་པར་དུ་ལོ་མཇུག་གི་དགུ་གཏོར་དང་། ཟླ་རེའི་ནང་གི་ནག་པོའི་ཕྱོགས་ཀྱི་མཐའ་མར་ཉེ་བའི་སྐབས་འདིར། དྲག་པོའི་ལས་སྒྲུབ་པ་དང་བར་ཆད་བཟློག་པའི་དུས་ཁྱད་པར་ཅན་ཞིག་ཏུ་ངོས་འཛིན་བྱེད།
༢། བྱེད་སྒོ།
ཉིན་འདིར་དགོན་སྡེ་ཁག་དང་ཁྱིམ་ཚང་སོ་སོའི་ནང་དུ།
 * གསོལ་ཁ་འབུལ་བ། ཆོས་སྐྱོང་སྲུང་མ་རྣམས་ལ་གཏོར་མ་དང་། གསེར་སྐྱེམས། བསངས་མཆོད་སོགས་རྒྱ་ཆེན་པོ་འབུལ་བ།
 * བསྐང་བཤགས་གཏོང་བ། རང་གི་དམ་ཚིག་ཉམས་ཆག་རྣམས་བཤགས་ཤིང་། སྲུང་མའི་ཐུགས་དམ་བསྐང་བའི་ཆོ་ག་བྱེད་པ།
༣། ཕན་ཡོན།
ཚེས་ ༢༩ ལ་སྲུང་མ་མཆོད་པའི་ཕན་ཡོན་ནི་བསམ་གྱིས་མི་ཁྱབ་པ་ཡོད་དེ།
 * བར་ཆད་ཞི་བ། ལམ་ལྷོངས་མ་ཡོང་བའི་གེགས་བར་དང་། མི་ཁ་ཁ་མཆུ། གནོད་པ་ཐམས་ཅད་ཞི་བར་བྱེད།
 * དམ་ཚིག་གཙང་མར་བཟོ་བ། བླ་མ་དང་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་དབར་གྱི་འགལ་འཁྲུལ་དང་དམ་ཚིག་ཉམས་པ་རྣམས་སོར་ཆུད་པར་བྱེད།
 * བྱ་བ་ལམ་འགྲོ། ཆོས་བཞིན་སྒྲུབ་པའི་ལས་དོན་ཐམས་ཅད་ལ་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་གིས་མགོན་སྐྱོབ་དང་ཕྱྭ་གཡང་སྩོལ་བར་བྱེད།
 * བསྟན་པ་སྲུང་བ། སྒེར་གྱི་དོན་ཙམ་མ་ཡིན་པར་སངས་རྒྱས་ཀྱི་བསྟན་པ་དང་འཛམ་གླིང་ཞི་བདེ་སྲུང་བའི་ནུས་པ་སྤེལ།
༤། དམིགས་བསལ་གྱི་དོན་སྙིང་།
སྲུང་མ་མཆོད་པ་ནི་ལྷ་གསོལ་བ་ཙམ་མ་ཡིན་པར། རང་གི་སེམས་ནང་གི་*དྲག་པོའི་ནུས་པ་*འཕེལ་བར་བྱས་ཏེ། ནང་གི་ཉོན་མོངས་པའི་དགྲ་བཅོམ་པ་དང་། ཕྱིའི་འཇིག་རྟེན་གྱི་བྱ་བ་གང་ཡང་བརྟན་པོར་སྐྱོང་བའི་སྙིང་སྟོབས་བསྐྱེད་པ་ལ་གོ་དགོས།
> ལམ་སྟོན། ཉིན་འདིར་ཁྱེད་ཀྱིས་རང་གི་ཡི་དམ་མམ་སྲུང་མའི་མ་ཎི་དང་སྙིང་པོ་བཟླས་པ་དང་། གཙང་བྲ་ལ་གཟབ་ནས་གསེར་སྐྱེམས་འབུལ་ན་ཤིན་ཏུ་ལེགས།"
Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.
Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts
1st Event: Birthday of His Holiness Dudjom Yangsi Tenzin Yeshe Dorje Rinpoche.
1st Event's Details:
Brief Long-Life Prayer for Kyabje Dudjom Yangsi Tenzin Yeshi Dorjee Rinpoche.
Rig-kün khyab-dag dö-mä gön-po dang
To the Primordial Protector, the All-pervading Lord of all Buddha families,
Yer-me Tän-dzin Ye-she Dor-je chog
Inseparable from the supreme Tenzin Yeshe Dorje,
Sang-sum dor-je'i tri la zhab-tän nä
May your secret Three Vajras (Body, Speech, and Mind) remain firm upon the Vajra Throne,
Thrin-lä nam-khä tha la khyab gyur chig
And may your enlightened activities pervade the furthest reaches of space!

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A day of introspection and renewal, traditionally used for purification practices and restoring commitments.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane:
"The Day of the Hundred Sacred Peaceful and Wrathful Deities"

Confession of Negative Actions (Digpa Shagpa): As it is the final day of the month, it is a day for purifying and confessing the non-virtuous actions committed throughout that month.
Restoration and Purification (Sojong): It is also the day when the monastic community (Sangha) gathers to perform the "Sojong" ritual, a ceremony for restoring vows and purifying transgressions according to the Vinaya (monastic discipline).
Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings grief and sorrow.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to strive towards the goal, Which is the supreme state of changeless, everlasting liberation, Since all the happiness of the three realms lasts but a moment, And then is quickly gone, just like dewdrops on blades of grass.
Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to arouse bodhicitta, So as to bring freedom to all sentient beings, infinite in number. For how can true happiness ever be found while our mothers, Who have cared for us throughout the ages, endure such pain?
Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to make a genuine exchange Of one’s own happiness and well-being for all the sufferings of others. Since all misery comes from seeking happiness for oneself alone, Whilst perfect buddhahood is born from the wish for others’ good.
Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.
1st Event: Parinirvana of H.H. Kathok Gertse Rinpoche
1st Event's Details:
Supplication to the 4th Katok Getse Rinpoche
Master of the entire Doctrine of the Buddha, the Katokpa,
Upholder of the Victory Banner of the Teachings with Unchanging Activity,
Crown Ornament of millions of scholars and realized masters, the Getsepa,
To the Fearless Lord of Speech, I pray!
This beautiful supplication (gsol 'debs) for the 4th H.H. Katok Getse Rinpoche, Gyurme Tenpa Gyaltsen, was composed by the H.H. Kyabje Dilgo Khyentse Rinpoche
1st Event's Details (Tibetan):
ཀཿ་ཐོག་དགེ་རྩེ་རིན་པོ་ཆེའི་གསོལ་འདེབས།
ཐུབ་བསྟན་ཡོངས་རྫོགས་མངའ་བདག་ཀཿ་ཐོག་པ། །
འགྱུར་མེད་ཕྲིན་ལས་བསྟན་པའི་རྒྱལ་མཚན་འཛིན། །
མཁས་གྲུབ་བྱེ་བའི་གཙུག་རྒྱན་དགེ་རྩེ་བ། །
འཇིགས་མེད་ངག་གི་དབང་པོར་གསོལ་བ་འདེབས། །


ཀཿ་ཐོག་དགེ་རྩེ་རིན་པོ་ཆེ་སྐུ་ཕྲེང་བཞི་པ་འགྱུར་མེད་བསྟན་པ་རྒྱལ་མཚན་མཆོག་གི་གསོལ་འདེབས་ངོ་མཚར་ཅན་འདི་ནི། ༧སྐྱབས་རྗེ་དིལ་མགོ་མཁྱེན་བརྩེ་རིན་པོ་ཆེ་མཆོག་གིས་ལྗགས་རྩོམ་གནང་བ་ཞིག་ལགས་སོ།།
2nd Event: Book Launch Ceremony: Release of a publication on the "Three Skills" (teaching, debating, and writing) held on the memorial of the Omniscient Rigzin Jigme Lingpa (1729–1798).

Day Significane: Even if others, in the grips of great desire, should steal, Or encourage others to take away, all the wealth that I possess, To dedicate to them entirely my body, possessions and all my merits From the past, present and future— this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.

Day Significane: Even if others should seek to cut off my head, Though I’ve done them not the slightest wrong, To take upon myself, out of compassion, All the harms they have amassed—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings thieves and robbers.

Day Significane: Even if others should declare before the world All manner of unpleasant things about me, To speak only of their qualities in return, With a mind that’s filled with love—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Achievement: Fulfils wishes and brings blessings.

Day Significane: Even if others should expose my hidden faults or deride me When speaking amidst great gatherings of many people, To conceive of them as spiritual friends and to bow Before them in respect—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Achievement: Fulfils wishes and brings blessings.
1st Event: Birthday of Kyabje Kathok Situ Rinpoche
1st Event's Details:
Long Life Prayer for the Supreme Katok Situ Rinpoche

OM SWASTI | NGE DÖN CHÖ KYI DRÖN ME TONG BAR WAY
Om Swasti! Through the thousand-fold radiance of the Lamp of Definitive Dharma,
 ZHING DIR SHAY DRUB NANG WAY GO GYA JAY
You open a hundred doors of teaching and practice within this realm.
TRIN LAY KÜN TU KHYAB DAK NIR MA KA
All-pervading Lord of enlightened activities, Nirmaka (Emanation),
PAL DEN ZANG PO ZHAB PAY TAK TEN SOL
Glorious and Excellent One, may your lotus feet remain forever firm

Long Life Prayer (Zhabten) for the current incarnation of H.E.Katok Situ Rinpoche, composed by the late Great Master Chatral Sangye Dorje Rinpoche
1st Event's Details (Tibetan):
"དཔལ་ཀཿ་ཐོག་སི་ཏུ་རིན་པོ་ཆེ་མཆོག་གི་ཞབས་བརྟན་བཞུགས་སོ།།

ཞེས་དཔལ་ཀཿཐོག་སི་ཏུ་རིན་པོ་ཆེའི་ཡང་སྲིད་མཆོག་གི་ཞབས་བརྟན་མཚན་གསོལ་དང་འབྲེལ་བའི་སྨོན་ཚིག་ཤྭ་ལོ་ཀ་འདི་ནི་རིག་པ་འཛིན་པ་སངས་རྒྱས་རྡོ་རྗེས་འབྲུག་ཕུན་ཚོགས་གླིང་ཕོ་བྲང་དུ་རབ་བྱུང་བཅུ་བདུན་པ་ཐོག་མ་གསར་དུ་ཚེས་པའི་ལོའི་རྒྱལ་ཟླ་བའི་ཡར་ཚེས་དགེ་བར་སྦྱར་བའོ། །
"
2nd Event: Anniversary of the Congressional Gold Medal: Commemorating the day the US Congress presented the Gold Medal to His Holiness the Dalai Lama.

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A day dedicated to healing prayers, longevity practices, and the cultivation of compassion for those who are ill.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane:
Bhagavan, whose compassion is equal toward all,
Just hearing your name clears the suffering of lower realms;
Medicine Buddha, healer of the diseases of the Three Poisons,
To you, the Light of Lapis Lazuli, I prostrate.
Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; very negative coincidence.

Lunar Status: The (Tibetan Day) is doubled this month
Day Significane: Even if others whom I have cared for like children of my own Should turn upon me and treat me as an enemy, To regard them only with special fondness and affection, As a mother would her ailing child—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Ambrosia: Strengthens healthy long life; good for business.
Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

Lunar Status: The (Tibetan day) is doubled this month
Day Significane: Even if others, equal or inferior to me in status, Should, out of arrogance, disparage me, To honour them, as I would my teacher, By bowing down my head before them—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.
Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A sacred day to honor Guru Padmasambhava, strengthen guru devotion, and renew Vajrayana commitments.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane:
Guru Padmasambhava
On the tenth day of the rat month, you tame the gods and spirits of Tibet,
You build Samyé to perfection, and light the flame of the sacred Dharma,
You ripen and liberate your fortunate disciples, the king and subjects
Padmasambhava, to you we pray.
 Grant your blessing so that idle chatter, harsh words, lies and slander,
Impairments of the śrāvaka’ vinaya discipline and bodhisattva precepts,
Breakages of samaya vows and the like, wrongdoing and downfalls, all are healed,
And our whole being is transformed and purified.

1st Event's Details:
Long Life Prayer for Tulku Namgyal Dawa Rinpoche

OM SWASTI | RAB JAM TSA SUM GYAL WAY JIN LAP KYI
 Om Swasti! Through the blessings of the infinite Three Roots and the Victorious Ones,
DÜ ZHI PUNG LAY NAM PAR GYAL WA YI
May you be completely victorious over the hosts of the four Maras (demons).
DRI DRAL DA WA TA BUI SANG WA SUM 
May your Three Secrets (Body, Speech, and Mind), stainless like the moon,
CHI ME GÖN POY CHAY POR TAK ZHUK NAY 
Remain forever firm as the companion of the Lord of Deathless Life (Amitayus).
KHA NYAM TEN DROY PHEN DEY PAL DU SHOK 
May you remain as the glorious benefit and happiness of the teachings and beings, vast as space!
~At the request of Tulku Tsephel, who offered a divine scarf (khata) and symbolic supports, this prayer of aspiration and supplication was written by the Buddhist monk Wagindra Dharma Mati (Khenpo Appey). May there be accomplishment! Siddhi Rastu!

Meaning of Coincidence: Achievement: Fulfils wishes and brings blessings.

1st Event: Lhabab Duchen: The Festival of the Buddha's Descent from Heaven
1st Event's Details:
Lhabab Duchen is one of the four great festivals (Chotrul Duchen, Saga Dawa, Chokhor Duchen, and Lhabab Duchen) in the Buddhist calendar.
1. The Essence of the Festival
Lhabab Duchen falls on the 22nd day of the 9th Tibetan month. It commemorates the occasion when Buddha Shakyamuni descended back to Earth in the city of Sankissa (Salden).
The Buddha had traveled to the Heaven of the Thirty-Three (Trāyastriṃśa) to give teachings to the gods, most notably to his mother, Mayadevi, to liberate her from cyclic existence and repay her kindness. After spending the summer retreat (Vassa) there, he returned to the human realm.
2. The Spiritual Benefits (Merit)
According to Buddhist tradition, the effects of both positive and negative actions are immensely multiplied on this day:
 * 100 Million-Fold Merit: It is believed that the merit from any virtuous deed performed on this day is multiplied 100,000,000 times.
 * Purification of Negativity: It is an exceptionally powerful time to practice purification rituals to cleanse past negative karma and downfalls.
3. Traditional Practices: What to do on this day?
To increase their roots of virtue, devotees traditionally engage in the following practices:
 * Nyungne (Fasting): Taking the Mahayana Eight Precepts, which involves a one-day vow of eating only vegetarian food and, in some cases, maintaining silence.
 * Offerings: Lighting butter lamps at temples and stupas, and performing circumambulations (Kora).
 * Recitations: Chanting praises of the Buddha’s miracles and reciting the King of Prayers (Samantabhadra Wishing Prayer).
 * Altar Preparations: Setting up elaborate offerings and white tormas (ritual cakes) in home altars to commemorate the Buddha's descent.

ཉིན་འདིར་དགོན་སྡེ་ཁག་དང་ཁྱིམ་ཚང་སོ་སོའི་ནང་དུ།
 * གསོལ་ཁ་འབུལ་བ། ཆོས་སྐྱོང་སྲུང་མ་རྣམས་ལ་གཏོར་མ་དང་། གསེར་སྐྱེམས། བསངས་མཆོད་སོགས་རྒྱ་ཆེན་པོ་འབུལ་བ།
 * བསྐང་བཤགས་གཏོང་བ། རང་གི་དམ་ཚིག་ཉམས་ཆག་རྣམས་བཤགས་ཤིང་། སྲུང་མའི་ཐུགས་དམ་བསྐང་བའི་ཆོ་ག་བྱེད་པ།
༣། ཕན་ཡོན།
ཚེས་ ༢༩ ལ་སྲུང་མ་མཆོད་པའི་ཕན་ཡོན་ནི་བསམ་གྱིས་མི་ཁྱབ་པ་ཡོད་དེ།
 * བར་ཆད་ཞི་བ། ལམ་ལྷོངས་མ་ཡོང་བའི་གེགས་བར་དང་། མི་ཁ་ཁ་མཆུ། གནོད་པ་ཐམས་ཅད་ཞི་བར་བྱེད།
 * དམ་ཚིག་གཙང་མར་བཟོ་བ། བླ་མ་དང་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་དབར་གྱི་འགལ་འཁྲུལ་དང་དམ་ཚིག་ཉམས་པ་རྣམས་སོར་ཆུད་པར་བྱེད།
 * བྱ་བ་ལམ་འགྲོ། ཆོས་བཞིན་སྒྲུབ་པའི་ལས་དོན་ཐམས་ཅད་ལ་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་གིས་མགོན་སྐྱོབ་དང་ཕྱྭ་གཡང་སྩོལ་བར་བྱེད།
 * བསྟན་པ་སྲུང་བ། སྒེར་གྱི་དོན་ཙམ་མ་ཡིན་པར་སངས་རྒྱས་ཀྱི་བསྟན་པ་དང་འཛམ་གླིང་ཞི་བདེ་སྲུང་བའི་ནུས་པ་སྤེལ།
༤། དམིགས་བསལ་གྱི་དོན་སྙིང་།
སྲུང་མ་མཆོད་པ་ནི་ལྷ་གསོལ་བ་ཙམ་མ་ཡིན་པར། རང་གི་སེམས་ནང་གི་*དྲག་པོའི་ནུས་པ་*འཕེལ་བར་བྱས་ཏེ། ནང་གི་ཉོན་མོངས་པའི་དགྲ་བཅོམ་པ་དང་། ཕྱིའི་འཇིག་རྟེན་གྱི་བྱ་བ་གང་ཡང་བརྟན་པོར་སྐྱོང་བའི་སྙིང་སྟོབས་བསྐྱེད་པ་ལ་གོ་དགོས།
> ལམ་སྟོན། ཉིན་འདིར་ཁྱེད་ཀྱིས་རང་གི་ཡི་དམ་མམ་སྲུང་མའི་མ་ཎི་དང་སྙིང་པོ་བཟླས་པ་དང་། གཙང་བྲ་ལ་གཟབ་ནས་གསེར་སྐྱེམས་འབུལ་ན་ཤིན་ཏུ་ལེགས།"
Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.
Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A day of introspection and renewal, traditionally used for purification practices and restoring commitments.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane:
"The Day of the Hundred Sacred Peaceful and Wrathful Deities"

Confession of Negative Actions (Digpa Shagpa): As it is the final day of the month, it is a day for purifying and confessing the non-virtuous actions committed throughout that month.
Restoration and Purification (Sojong): It is also the day when the monastic community (Sangha) gathers to perform the "Sojong" ritual, a ceremony for restoring vows and purifying transgressions according to the Vinaya (monastic discipline).
Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to take to solitary places, Avoiding the unwholesome, so that destructive emotions gradually fade away, And, in the absence of distraction, virtuous practice naturally gains strength; Whilst, with awareness clearly focused, we gain conviction in the teachings.
Meaning of Coincidence: Ample Food/Clothing: Good for giving generously.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to renounce this life’s concerns, For friends and relatives, long acquainted, must all go their separate ways; Wealth and prized possessions, painstakingly acquired, must all be left behind; And consciousness, the guest who lodges in the body, must in time depart.
Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to avoid destructive friends, In whose company the three poisons of the mind grow stronger, And we engage less and less in study, reflection and meditation, So that love and compassion fade away until they are no more.
Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings thieves and robbers.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cherish spiritual friends, By regarding them as even more precious than one’s own body, Since they are the ones who will help to rid us of all our faults, And make our virtues grow ever greater just like the waxing moon.
Meaning of Coincidence: Achievement: Fulfils wishes and brings blessings.
1st Event: First Examination at Odisha Dudjom Vihara

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to take refuge in the Three Jewels, Since they will never fail to provide protection for all who call upon them, For whom are the ordinary gods of this world ever capable of helping, As long as they themselves are trapped within saṃsāra’s vicious cycle?
Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is never to commit a harmful act, Even though not to do so might put one’s very life at risk, For the Sage himself has taught how negative actions will ripen Into the manifold miseries of the lower realms, so difficult to endure.
Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; very negative coincidence.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to strive towards the goal, Which is the supreme state of changeless, everlasting liberation, Since all the happiness of the three realms lasts but a moment, And then is quickly gone, just like dewdrops on blades of grass.
Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A day dedicated to healing prayers, longevity practices, and the cultivation of compassion for those who are ill.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane:
Bhagavan, whose compassion is equal toward all,
Just hearing your name clears the suffering of lower realms;
Medicine Buddha, healer of the diseases of the Three Poisons,
To you, the Light of Lapis Lazuli, I prostrate.
Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to arouse bodhicitta, So as to bring freedom to all sentient beings, infinite in number. For how can true happiness ever be found while our mothers, Who have cared for us throughout the ages, endure such pain?
Meaning of Coincidence: Ambrosia: Strengthens healthy long life; good for business.
Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A sacred day to honor Guru Padmasambhava, strengthen guru devotion, and renew Vajrayana commitments.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane:
Guru Dorjé Drolö
On the tenth day of the ox month, master and disciples confer,
And in the centre and on the borders of the land of snows you conceal millions of terma troves.
With these treasures you safeguard what remains of the teachings, all with your constant kindness
All-powerful Dorje Drolö, to you we pray.
Grant your blessing so that sudden misfortune, like untimely death, never befalls us,
And as soon as we transfer to the next life, we are born in the pure realm of Great Bliss,
And in the paradise of Lotus Light, in your very presence, Guru,
And become a source of limitless benefit to living beings.
Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings thieves and robbers.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to make a genuine exchange Of one’s own happiness and well-being for all the sufferings of others. Since all misery comes from seeking happiness for oneself alone, Whilst perfect buddhahood is born from the wish for others’ good.
Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.
1st Event: Second Examination at Odisha Dudjom Vihara

Day Significane: Even if others, in the grips of great desire, should steal, Or encourage others to take away, all the wealth that I possess, To dedicate to them entirely my body, possessions and all my merits From the past, present and future— this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

Day Significane: Even if others should seek to cut off my head, Though I’ve done them not the slightest wrong, To take upon myself, out of compassion, All the harms they have amassed—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; very negative coincidence.

Day Significane: Even if others should declare before the world All manner of unpleasant things about me, To speak only of their qualities in return, With a mind that’s filled with love—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A powerful day for merit-making, purification, offerings, and strengthening positive intentions.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane: "The Day of Amitabha Buddha"
Meaning of Coincidence: Ample Food/Clothing: Good for giving generously.

Day Significane: Even if others should expose my hidden faults or deride me When speaking amidst great gatherings of many people, To conceive of them as spiritual friends and to bow Before them in respect—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.
1st Event: Third Examination at Odisha Dudjom Vihara

Day Significane: Even if others whom I have cared for like children of my own Should turn upon me and treat me as an enemy, To regard them only with special fondness and affection, As a mother would her ailing child—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings thieves and robbers.

Day Significane: Even if others, equal or inferior to me in status, Should, out of arrogance, disparage me, To honour them, as I would my teacher, By bowing down my head before them—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings grief and sorrow.

Day Significane: Even though I may be destitute and despised by all, Beset with terrible illness and plagued by evil spirits, Still to take upon myself all beings’ ills and harmful actions, Without ever losing heart—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.
Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

Day Significane: Even though I may be famous and revered by all, And as rich as Vaiśravaṇa, the god of wealth himself, To see the futility of all the glory and riches of this world And to remain without conceit—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to subdue the mind With the forces of loving kindness and compassion. For unless the real adversary—my own anger—is defeated, Outer enemies, though I may conquer them, will continue to appear.
Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.
1st Event: Fourth Examination at Odisha Dudjom Vihara

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to turn away immediately From those things which bring desire and attachment. For the pleasures of the senses are just like salty water: The more we taste of them, the more our thirst increases.
Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; very negative coincidence.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is never to entertain concepts, Which revolve around dualistic notions of perceiver and perceived, In the knowledge that all these appearances are but the mind itself, Whilst mind’s own nature is forever beyond the limitations of ideas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.
1st Event: Anniversary of the Nobel Peace Prize: Commemorating the award presented to His Holiness the Dalai Lama.

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A day connected to wisdom energy and enlightened activity — suitable for mantra recitation and inner practice.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane:
"The Day of the Dakini Festival"                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          Mother of all the victorious ones, Vajrayoginī,
Mother of diversity, Prajñāpāramitā,
Padma's consort, joyful, passionate and smiling—
Yeshe Tsogyal, homage to you!
Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to let go of grasping When encountering things one finds pleasant or attractive, Considering them to be like rainbows in the summer skies— Beautiful in appearance, yet in truth devoid of any substance.
Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings grief and sorrow.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to recognize delusion Whenever one is confronted by adversity or misfortune. For these sufferings are just like the death of a child in a dream, And it’s so exhausting to cling to delusory perceptions as real.
Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings grief and sorrow.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to give out of generosity, With no hopes of karmic recompense or expectation of reward. For if those who seek awakening must give even their own bodies, What need is there to mention mere outer objects and possessions?
Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.
1st Event: Fifth Examination at Odisha Dudjom Vihara

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A day to make offerings and prayers to Dharma Protectors for the removal of obstacles and protection of practice.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane: Day dedicated to invoking Dharma Protectors like Mahakala and Palden Lhamo to remove obstacles and strengthen protection.
Unnamed: 25:
"བོད་ཟླ་རེའི་ཚེས་ ༢༩ ཉིན་ནི་*ཆོས་སྐྱོང་སྲུང་མའི་དུས་ཆེན་ནམ་དུས་བཟང་*གལ་ཆེན་ཞིག་ཡིན། 
༡། གོ་དོན།
བོད་ཀྱི་ལོ་ཐོའི་ནང་ཟླ་རེའི་ཚེས་ ༢༩ ནི་*ཡི་དམ་དང་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་*རྣམས་ཀྱི་དུས་བཟང་དུ་བརྩི་བ་ཡིན། ལྷག་པར་དུ་ལོ་མཇུག་གི་དགུ་གཏོར་དང་། ཟླ་རེའི་ནང་གི་ནག་པོའི་ཕྱོགས་ཀྱི་མཐའ་མར་ཉེ་བའི་སྐབས་འདིར། དྲག་པོའི་ལས་སྒྲུབ་པ་དང་བར་ཆད་བཟློག་པའི་དུས་ཁྱད་པར་ཅན་ཞིག་ཏུ་ངོས་འཛིན་བྱེད།
༢། བྱེད་སྒོ།
ཉིན་འདིར་དགོན་སྡེ་ཁག་དང་ཁྱིམ་ཚང་སོ་སོའི་ནང་དུ།
 * གསོལ་ཁ་འབུལ་བ། ཆོས་སྐྱོང་སྲུང་མ་རྣམས་ལ་གཏོར་མ་དང་། གསེར་སྐྱེམས། བསངས་མཆོད་སོགས་རྒྱ་ཆེན་པོ་འབུལ་བ།
 * བསྐང་བཤགས་གཏོང་བ། རང་གི་དམ་ཚིག་ཉམས་ཆག་རྣམས་བཤགས་ཤིང་། སྲུང་མའི་ཐུགས་དམ་བསྐང་བའི་ཆོ་ག་བྱེད་པ།
༣། ཕན་ཡོན།
ཚེས་ ༢༩ ལ་སྲུང་མ་མཆོད་པའི་ཕན་ཡོན་ནི་བསམ་གྱིས་མི་ཁྱབ་པ་ཡོད་དེ།
 * བར་ཆད་ཞི་བ། ལམ་ལྷོངས་མ་ཡོང་བའི་གེགས་བར་དང་། མི་ཁ་ཁ་མཆུ། གནོད་པ་ཐམས་ཅད་ཞི་བར་བྱེད།
 * དམ་ཚིག་གཙང་མར་བཟོ་བ། བླ་མ་དང་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་དབར་གྱི་འགལ་འཁྲུལ་དང་དམ་ཚིག་ཉམས་པ་རྣམས་སོར་ཆུད་པར་བྱེད།
 * བྱ་བ་ལམ་འགྲོ། ཆོས་བཞིན་སྒྲུབ་པའི་ལས་དོན་ཐམས་ཅད་ལ་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་གིས་མགོན་སྐྱོབ་དང་ཕྱྭ་གཡང་སྩོལ་བར་བྱེད།
 * བསྟན་པ་སྲུང་བ། སྒེར་གྱི་དོན་ཙམ་མ་ཡིན་པར་སངས་རྒྱས་ཀྱི་བསྟན་པ་དང་འཛམ་གླིང་ཞི་བདེ་སྲུང་བའི་ནུས་པ་སྤེལ།
༤། དམིགས་བསལ་གྱི་དོན་སྙིང་།

1st Event: The Nine Bad Omens (Ngenpa Gu Dzom)"
1st Event's Details:
The Nine Bad Omens (Ngenpa Gu Dzom)
Although there are several different interpretations of how to identify the Nine Bad Omens, according to the traditional systems of Tibetan astrology, they are generally categorized into three groups based on the stars in the sky, the elements on the earth, and the directions in space.
1. The Three Bad Omens of the Sky
These three bad omens arise from the alignment of the planets and the constellations:
* Bad Planets: Regardless of whether the day is a Thursday or a Friday (usually considered auspicious), it is calculated as an unfavorable planetary day where activities will not succeed.
* Bad Constellations: An unfavorable constellation from among the 28 lunar mansions falls on this day.
* Bad Timing: Certain periods or hours of that day are considered inauspicious for any kind of activity.
2. The Three Bad Omens of the Earth
These relate to the elements of the ground and the cardinal directions:
* Incompatible Elements: The elements—Earth, Water, Fire, and Air—clash with one another (for example, Fire and Water meeting).
* Bad Directions: The alignment points toward directions where the "Gates of Heaven" or "Gates of Earth" are said to be open (traditionally seen as risky or unstable).
* Hostile Earth-Lords: The local spirits and "Earth-Lords" (Sa-dak) are considered agitated, making it unsuitable to perform any earthworks or stonework.
3. The Three Intermediate Bad Omens
These refer to the connection between humans and their intended actions:
* Lack of Progress: It is considered an unlucky time for travel, business, or commerce.
* Broken Interdependence: Auspicious ceremonies like weddings or celebrations are likely to face obstacles or bad luck.
* Unmet Intentions: Even if one has good intentions, the results are expected to be poor or unsuccessful.
In Summary
According to the Tibetan calendar, the 6th day of the 11th Tibetan month is the actual day of "Ngenpa Gu Dzom." On this day, Tibetan people avoid starting important work, stay home to rest, and refrain from offering ceremonial scarves (khata)

1st Event: The Ten Auspicious Omens (Zangpo Chu Dzom)
1st Event's Details:
The Ten Auspicious Omens (Zangpo Chu Dzom)
In Tibetan astrology, Zangpo Chu Dzom is considered an extremely virtuous and positive day. It falls on the 7th day of the 11th Tibetan month, which is the day immediately following "Ngenpa Gu Dzom" (The Nine Bad Omens).
Below are the detailed explanations and benefits of this day:
1. What are the Ten Auspicious Omens?
The term "Ten Auspicious Omens" refers to the gathering of ten harmonious conditions involving gods, spirits (Nagas), humans, elements, and planets. They are categorized as follows:
* The Three Good Omens of the Sky: (Auspicious planets, auspicious constellations, and auspicious timing.)
* The Three Good Omens of the Earth: (Auspicious directions, harmonious elements, and pleased Earth-Lords.)
* The Three Intermediate Good Omens: (Perfect interdependent connections, fulfillment of intentions, and favorable circumstances for tasks.)
* The One General Auspicious Condition: (The harmonious connection between gods and humans.)
2. Benefits and Virtues of This Day
It is said that any activity started on the day of the Ten Auspicious Omens will succeed without obstacles.
* Success in Activities: Important tasks such as business ventures, marriages, and building new houses are blessed and will reach a successful conclusion.
* Increase of Merit: Performing virtuous deeds and making offerings on this day is believed to multiply one's merit manifold.
* Clearing of Negativity: It is said that the "pollution" and negativity from the previous day (Ngenpa Gu Dzom) are purified, allowing auspicious glory to shine.
* Physical and Mental Well-being: It is a positive time when people naturally feel more harmonious and develop virtuous thoughts.
3. Special Customs and Activities
Tibetan people traditionally observe this day with specific activities:
* Offering Khata: Giving ceremonial scarves as a symbol of auspicious beginnings and interdependence.
* Lhasol and Sang-sol: Offering prayers to local deities and performing smoke purification (Sang) ceremonies.
* Starting New Ventures: If there is a new project to begin, this day is specifically chosen for its favorable energy.
* Celebrations: Families and relatives gather to enjoy festive meals and celebrate together.
In Summary
The transition from the bad timing of "Ngenpa Gu Dzom" to the auspicious timing of "Zangpo Chu Dzom" is viewed as a symbolic event, much like "the sun rising after a period of thick darkness."

1. གནམ་གྱི་བཟང་པོ་གསུམ། (གཟའ་བཟང་། སྐར་མ་བཟང་། དུས་ཚོད་བཟང་བ།)
2. སའི་བཟང་པོ་གསུམ། (ཕྱོགས་བཟང་། འབྱུང་བ་མཐུན་པ། ས་བདག་དགྱེས་པ།)
3. བར་གྱི་བཟང་པོ་གསུམ། (རྟེན་འབྲེལ་འགྲིག་པ། བསམ་དོན་འགྲུབ་པ། ལས་སྣེ་ལེགས་པ།)
4. སྤྱིའི་བཀྲ་ཤིས་པའི་ཆ་རྐྱེན་གཅིག (ལྷ་མི་ཀུན་མཐུན་གྱི་རྟེན་འབྲེལ།)
༢. ཉིན་མོ་འདིའི་ཕན་ཡོན་དང་དགེ་མཚན།
བཟང་པོ་བཅུ་འཛོམས་ཀྱི་ཉིན་མོར་བྱ་བ་གང་ཞིག་བརྩམས་ཀྱང་བར་ཆད་མེད་པར་ལམ་དུ་འགྲོ་བའི་ཕན་ཡོན་ཡོད་པར་བཤད།
* བྱ་བ་ལམ་ལྷོང། ཚོང་ལས་དང་། གཉེན་སྒྲིག ཁང་པ་གསར་རྒྱག་སོགས་གལ་ཆེའི་ལས་ཀ་གང་བྱས་ཀྱང་བཀྲ་ཤིས་ཤིང་དོན་འགྲུབ་པ།
* བསོད་ནམས་འཕེལ་བ། དགེ་བའི་ལས་དང་མཆོད་འབུལ་བྱས་ན་བསོད་ནམས་ལྡབ་འགྱུར་གྱིས་འཕེལ་བར་བརྩི།
* རྐྱེན་ངན་སེལ་བ། སྔོན་གྱི་ཉིན་མོ་ (ངན་པ་དགུ་འཛོམས) ཀྱི་གྲིབ་དང་མི་གཙང་བ་ཐམས་ཅད་དག་ནས་བཀྲ་ཤིས་པའི་དཔལ་འབར་བར་བཤད།
* ལུས་སེམས་བདེ་བ། མི་རྣམས་ཕན་ཚུན་མཐུན་ལམ་དང་། བསམ་པ་བཟང་པོ་སྐྱེ་བའི་དུས་བཟང་ཞིག་ཡིན།
༣. དམིགས་བསལ་གྱི་བྱེད་སྒོ།
བོད་མི་ཚོས་ཉིན་མོ་འདིར་དམིགས་བསལ་གྱིས་གཤམ་གསལ་གྱི་ལས་རྣམས་བྱེད་སྲོལ་ཡོད།
* ཁ་བཏགས་གཏོང་བ། བཀྲ་ཤིས་པའི་རྟེན་འབྲེལ་མཚོན་བྱེད་དུ་ཁ་བཏགས་འབུལ་བ།
* ལྷ་གསོལ་བསངས་གཏོང་། ཡུལ་ལྷ་གཞི་བདག་མཆོད་པ་དང་བསངས་གསོལ་བྱེད་པ།
* བྱ་བ་གསར་པ་འགོ་འཛུགས། ལས་ཀ་གང་ཞིག་འགོ་འཛུགས་རྒྱུ་ཡོད་ན་ཉིན་འདིར་བདམས་ནས་བྱེད་པ།
* དགའ་སྟོན་བྱེད་པ། ནང་མི་སྤུན་ཉེ་མཉམ་འཛོམས་ཀྱིས་སྐྱིད་པོ་བྱེད་པ།
མདོར་ན། ངན་པ་དགུ་འཛོམས་ཀྱི་དུས་ཚོད་ངན་པ་དེ་ཡོལ་བ་དང་། བཟང་པོ་བཅུ་འཛོམས་ཀྱི་བཀྲ་ཤིས་པའི་དུས་ཚོད་འགོ་ཚུགས་པ་ནི། "མུན་པ་འཐིབས་པའི་རྗེས་སུ་ཉི་མ་ཤར་བ་" ལྟ་བུའི་རྟེན་འབྲེལ་ཞིག་ལ་ངོས་འཛིན་བྱེད་ཀྱི་ཡོད།

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A day dedicated to healing prayers, longevity practices, and the cultivation of compassion for those who are ill.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane:
Bhagavan, whose compassion is equal toward all,
Just hearing your name clears the suffering of lower realms;
Medicine Buddha, healer of the diseases of the Three Poisons,
To you, the Light of Lapis Lazuli, I prostrate.
Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings thieves and robbers.
1st Event: Parinirvana of Trakthung Dudjom Lingpa (Dudjom Tersar lineage).

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to renounce this life’s concerns, For friends and relatives, long acquainted, must all go their separate ways; Wealth and prized possessions, painstakingly acquired, must all be left behind; And consciousness, the guest who lodges in the body, must in time depart.
Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.
Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A sacred day to honor Guru Padmasambhava, strengthen guru devotion, and renew Vajrayana commitments.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane:
Guru Pema Gyalpo
On the tenth day of the tiger month, you arrive at the palace in Uḍḍiyāna,
And are adopted by Indrabhūti as his crown prince.
Taking Prabhāvatī as your wife, you rule as a universal monarch—
Guru Pema Gyalpo, to you we pray.
Grant your blessing so that our brilliance, charisma and strength increase, epidemics and turmoil in the elements are pacified,
The five senses become pliable, and happiness, peace of mind and confidence quickly arise,
We gather under our power the prāṇa-mind of humans and non-humans,
And all our aspirations are fulfilled, just as we desire.
Unnamed: 25:
སྟག་ཟླའི་ཚེས་བཅུར་ཨོ་རྒྱན་རྒྱལ་སར་ཕེབས། །
ཨིནྡྲ་བྷཱུ་ཏིའི་སྲས་ཀྱི་སྐལ་པར་མཛད། །
འོད་འཆང་ཁབ་བཞེས་འཁོར་ལོས་སྒྱུར་རྒྱལ་གྱུར། །
གུ་རུ་པདྨ་རྒྱལ་པོར་གསོལ་བ་འདེབས། །
གཟི་བརྗིད་སྟོབས་རྒྱས་ནད་རིགས་འབྱུང་འཁྲུགས་ཞི། །
དབང་ལྔ་ལས་རུང་ཡིད་བདེ་སྤོབས་པ་མྱུར། །
མི་དང་མི་མིན་རླུང་སེམས་དབང་དུ་འདུས། །
བསམ་དོན་ཡིད་བཞིན་འགྲུབ་པར་བྱིན་གྱིས་རློབས། །
Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to avoid destructive friends, In whose company the three poisons of the mind grow stronger, And we engage less and less in study, reflection and meditation, So that love and compassion fade away until they are no more.
Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; very negative coincidence.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cherish spiritual friends, By regarding them as even more precious than one’s own body, Since they are the ones who will help to rid us of all our faults, And make our virtues grow ever greater just like the waxing moon.
Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.
1st Event: Parinirvana of Kyabje Dudjom Jikdrel Yeshe Dorje Rinpoche (1904–1987)

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to take refuge in the Three Jewels, Since they will never fail to provide protection for all who call upon them, For whom are the ordinary gods of this world ever capable of helping, As long as they themselves are trapped within saṃsāra’s vicious cycle?
Meaning of Coincidence: Ample Food/Clothing: Good for giving generously.
1st Event: Parinirvana of Kyabje Taklung Tsetrul Rinpoche (Dorje Drak, 1926–2015).

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is never to commit a harmful act, Even though not to do so might put one’s very life at risk, For the Sage himself has taught how negative actions will ripen Into the manifold miseries of the lower realms, so difficult to endure.
Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to strive towards the goal, Which is the supreme state of changeless, everlasting liberation, Since all the happiness of the three realms lasts but a moment, And then is quickly gone, just like dewdrops on blades of grass.
Meaning of Coincidence: Certainty: Fulfills wishes speedily; resolves disputes.
1st Event: Parinirvana of Khenchen Jigme Phuntsok (Palyul lineage, 1933–2004).

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to arouse bodhicitta, So as to bring freedom to all sentient beings, infinite in number. For how can true happiness ever be found while our mothers, Who have cared for us throughout the ages, endure such pain?
Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to make a genuine exchange Of one’s own happiness and well-being for all the sufferings of others. Since all misery comes from seeking happiness for oneself alone, Whilst perfect buddhahood is born from the wish for others’ good.
Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings grief and sorrow.

Day Significane: Even if others, in the grips of great desire, should steal, Or encourage others to take away, all the wealth that I possess, To dedicate to them entirely my body, possessions and all my merits From the past, present and future— this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Ample Food/Clothing: Good for wealth practices.
Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts
1st Event: Annual Monastic Holiday: Commencement of the monks' annual vacation.

Day Significane: Even if others should seek to cut off my head, Though I’ve done them not the slightest wrong, To take upon myself, out of compassion, All the harms they have amassed—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Ambrosia: Strengthens healthy long life; good for business.
1st Event: Parinirvana of Chatral Sangye Dorje Rinpoche (Kathok lineage, 1913–2015).

Day Significane: Even if others should declare before the world All manner of unpleasant things about me, To speak only of their qualities in return, With a mind that’s filled with love—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Ample Food/Clothing: Good for wealth practices.

Day Significane: Even if others should expose my hidden faults or deride me When speaking amidst great gatherings of many people, To conceive of them as spiritual friends and to bow Before them in respect—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.

Day Significane: Even if others whom I have cared for like children of my own Should turn upon me and treat me as an enemy, To regard them only with special fondness and affection, As a mother would her ailing child—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Certainty: Fulfills wishes speedily; resolves disputes.
1st Event: Birthday of the 4th Kyabje Drubwang Rinpoche

Day Significane: Even if others, equal or inferior to me in status, Should, out of arrogance, disparage me, To honour them, as I would my teacher, By bowing down my head before them—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings grief and sorrow.

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A day connected to wisdom energy and enlightened activity — suitable for mantra recitation and inner practice.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane:
"The Day of the Dakini Festival"                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          Mother of all the victorious ones, Vajrayoginī,
Mother of diversity, Prajñāpāramitā,
Padma's consort, joyful, passionate and smiling—
Yeshe Tsogyal, homage to you!
Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings grief and sorrow.

Day Significane: Even though I may be destitute and despised by all, Beset with terrible illness and plagued by evil spirits, Still to take upon myself all beings’ ills and harmful actions, Without ever losing heart—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

Day Significane: Even though I may be famous and revered by all, And as rich as Vaiśravaṇa, the god of wealth himself, To see the futility of all the glory and riches of this world And to remain without conceit—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.

Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to subdue the mind With the forces of loving kindness and compassion. For unless the real adversary—my own anger—is defeated, Outer enemies, though I may conquer them, will continue to appear.
Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.

Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A day to make offerings and prayers to Dharma Protectors for the removal of obstacles and protection of practice.
Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
Day Significane: Day dedicated to invoking Dharma Protectors like Mahakala and Palden Lhamo to remove obstacles and strengthen protection.
Unnamed: 25:
"བོད་ཟླ་རེའི་ཚེས་ ༢༩ ཉིན་ནི་*ཆོས་སྐྱོང་སྲུང་མའི་དུས་ཆེན་ནམ་དུས་བཟང་*གལ་ཆེན་ཞིག་ཡིན། 
༡། གོ་དོན།
བོད་ཀྱི་ལོ་ཐོའི་ནང་ཟླ་རེའི་ཚེས་ ༢༩ ནི་*ཡི་དམ་དང་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་*རྣམས་ཀྱི་དུས་བཟང་དུ་བརྩི་བ་ཡིན། ལྷག་པར་དུ་ལོ་མཇུག་གི་དགུ་གཏོར་དང་། ཟླ་རེའི་ནང་གི་ནག་པོའི་ཕྱོགས་ཀྱི་མཐའ་མར་ཉེ་བའི་སྐབས་འདིར། དྲག་པོའི་ལས་སྒྲུབ་པ་དང་བར་ཆད་བཟློག་པའི་དུས་ཁྱད་པར་ཅན་ཞིག་ཏུ་ངོས་འཛིན་བྱེད།
༢། བྱེད་སྒོ།
ཉིན་འདིར་དགོན་སྡེ་ཁག་དང་ཁྱིམ་ཚང་སོ་སོའི་ནང་དུ།
 * གསོལ་ཁ་འབུལ་བ། ཆོས་སྐྱོང་སྲུང་མ་རྣམས་ལ་གཏོར་མ་དང་། གསེར་སྐྱེམས། བསངས་མཆོད་སོགས་རྒྱ་ཆེན་པོ་འབུལ་བ།
 * བསྐང་བཤགས་གཏོང་བ། རང་གི་དམ་ཚིག་ཉམས་ཆག་རྣམས་བཤགས་ཤིང་། སྲུང་མའི་ཐུགས་དམ་བསྐང་བའི་ཆོ་ག་བྱེད་པ།
༣། ཕན་ཡོན།
ཚེས་ ༢༩ ལ་སྲུང་མ་མཆོད་པའི་ཕན་ཡོན་ནི་བསམ་གྱིས་མི་ཁྱབ་པ་ཡོད་དེ།
 * བར་ཆད་ཞི་བ། ལམ་ལྷོངས་མ་ཡོང་བའི་གེགས་བར་དང་། མི་ཁ་ཁ་མཆུ། གནོད་པ་ཐམས་ཅད་ཞི་བར་བྱེད།
 * དམ་ཚིག་གཙང་མར་བཟོ་བ། བླ་མ་དང་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་དབར་གྱི་འགལ་འཁྲུལ་དང་དམ་ཚིག་ཉམས་པ་རྣམས་སོར་ཆུད་པར་བྱེད།

OM SWASTI | TSE LHA RIG DZIN GYA TSOY NGO WO NYID 
Om Swasti! You are the very essence of the ocean of Longevity Deities and Vidyadharas.
CHI ME TSO KYE DOR JE JIN LAP KYI 
Through the blessings of the Deathless Lake-Born Vajra (Guru Rinpoche),

1st Event: The Great Nyingma Prayer Festival (Nyingma Monlam Chenmo) in BodhGaya.
1st Event's Details:
The Nyingma Monlam Chenmo at Bodh Gaya is a major religious festival held annually in front of the Holy Mahabodhi Stupa in Bodh Gaya, India. Its primary purpose is to pray for world peace and the flourishing of the Buddha's teachings.
Key details about this event include:
* Timing: Traditionally, it is held for ten days, from the 1st to the 10th day of the 12th Tibetan month.
* Location: It takes place under the Bodhi Tree at Bodh Gaya, the supreme holy site where Shakyamuni Buddha attained complete enlightenment.
* Objective: To offer prayers for world peace, the well-being of all sentient beings, and specifically for the preservation and spread of the Early Translation School (Nyingma) teachings.
* Leadership: High lamas of the Nyingma lineage, Khenpos, Tulkus, and thousands of monks and nuns gather together for the ceremony.
What is primarily recited?
During this prayer festival, the main recitation is "The King of Aspiration Prayers: Samantabhadra’s Sevenfold Prayer" (Phagpa Zangpo Chöpay Mönlam Gyi Gyalpo). In addition to this, many other supplications and aspiration prayers from the lineage of the Awareness-Holders (Vidyadharas) are recited.
Significance for your projects
Given your involvement in creating booklets and coordinating with lamas for sacred ceremonies (such as the Guru Rinpoche Tsok Chöd), this festival is the pinnacle of such spiritual gatherings. The text you previously mentioned, "Kalsang Dö Jö", is perfectly aligned with the spirit of these Great Prayer Festivals.
